## 🤖 Claude


🔧 Using tool: Edit
Input: {
  "replace_all": false,
  "file_path": "/Users/cell/claude/tcc-darwin8-ppc/tcc/ppc-gen.c",
  "old_string": "/* Bounds-check support is deferred. tccgen.c references\n * func_bound_add_epilog unconditionally (not gated by\n * CONFIG_TCC_BCHECK), so its storage must always exist. The other\n * two are only used by future bcheck-aware codegen routines. */\nST_DATA int func_bound_add_epilog;\n#if defined(CONFIG_TCC_BCHECK)\nstatic addr_t func_bound_offset;\nstatic unsigned long func_bound_ind;\n#endif",
  "new_string": "/* Bounds-check support is deferred. tccgen.c references\n * func_bound_add_epilog unconditionally (not gated by\n * CONFIG_TCC_BCHECK), so its storage must always exist. The other\n * two are only used by future bcheck-aware codegen routines. */\nST_DATA int func_bound_add_epilog;\n#if defined(CONFIG_TCC_BCHECK)\nstatic addr_t func_bound_offset;\nstatic unsigned long func_bound_ind;\n#endif\n\n/* Function-prolog backfill bookkeeping. The prolog is reserved at\n * gfunc_prolog time but emitted at gfunc_epilog time, when we know\n * the final frame size. Mirrors i386-gen.c's pattern. */\nstatic unsigned long func_sub_sp_offset;\n\n/* Apple PPC ABI:\n *   - 24-byte linkage area at top of caller's frame (back-chain,\n *     saved CR, saved LR, three reserved words).\n *   - Stack alignment is 16 bytes.\n *   - Outgoing param area sits above linkage; we don't yet allocate\n *     extra space for it (no calls in v1 codegen).\n *\n * PROLOG_SIZE = 3 instructions = 12 bytes:\n *   mflr r0\n *   stw  r0, 8(r1)\n *   stwu r1, -frame_size(r1)\n */\n#define PPC_PROLOG_SIZE 12\n#define PPC_LINKAGE_SIZE 24\n#define PPC_STACK_ALIGN 16\n\nstatic int ppc_frame_size(void)\n{\n    /* loc is non-positive (locals grow downward). Frame holds\n     * linkage + locals, rounded up to 16. Minimum 32 keeps things\n     * sane for trivial leaf functions. */\n    int n = PPC_LINKAGE_SIZE + (-loc);\n    n = (n + PPC_STACK_ALIGN - 1) & -PPC_STACK_ALIGN;\n    if (n < 32) n = 32;\n    return n;\n}"
}

## 🤖 Claude


🔧 Using tool: Edit
Input: {
  "replace_all": false,
  "file_path": "/Users/cell/claude/tcc-darwin8-ppc/tcc/ppc-gen.c",
  "old_string": "ST_FUNC void load(int r, SValue *sv)\n{\n    tcc_error(\"ppc-gen: load stub (r=%d type=0x%x)\", r, sv ? sv->type.t : 0);\n}",
  "new_string": "/* Emit a load-immediate of a 32-bit value into GPR `gpr`. Uses\n *   li  rD, simm                  (one instruction, signed 16-bit)\n *   lis rD, hi                    (one instruction, low 16 zero)\n *   lis rD, hi  / ori rD, rD, lo  (two instructions, full 32-bit)\n */\nstatic void ppc_emit_li(int gpr, int32_t v)\n{\n    if (v >= -0x8000 && v < 0x8000) {\n        /* li rD, v  ->  addi rD, 0, v  (sign-extends) */\n        o(0x38000000 | (gpr << 21) | (v & 0xffff));\n    } else if ((v & 0xffff) == 0) {\n        /* lis rD, hi  ->  addis rD, 0, hi */\n        o(0x3c000000 | (gpr << 21) | ((v >> 16) & 0xffff));\n    } else {\n        /* lis rD, hi ; ori rD, rD, lo */\n        o(0x3c000000 | (gpr << 21) | ((v >> 16) & 0xffff));\n        o(0x60000000 | (gpr << 21) | (gpr << 16) | (v & 0xffff));\n    }\n}\n\nST_FUNC void load(int r, SValue *sv)\n{\n    int v = sv->r & VT_VALMASK;\n    int gpr;\n\n    /* v1: only handle integer constant immediate. Everything else\n     * stubs to a clear error message. */\n    if (v == VT_CONST && !(sv->r & VT_LVAL) && !(sv->r & VT_SYM)) {\n        if (IS_FREG(r)) {\n            tcc_error(\"ppc-gen: load FP constant not yet implemented\");\n        }\n        gpr = TREG_TO_GPR(r);\n        ppc_emit_li(gpr, (int32_t)sv->c.i);\n        return;\n    }\n\n    tcc_error(\"ppc-gen: load stub (r=%d sv->r=0x%x type=0x%x val=0x%llx)\",\n              r, sv->r, sv->type.t, (long long)sv->c.i);\n}"
}

## 🤖 Claude


🔧 Using tool: Edit
Input: {
  "replace_all": false,
  "file_path": "/Users/cell/claude/tcc-darwin8-ppc/tcc/ppc-gen.c",
  "old_string": "ST_FUNC void gfunc_prolog(Sym *func_sym)\n{\n    tcc_error(\"ppc-gen: gfunc_prolog stub\");\n}\n\nST_FUNC void gfunc_epilog(void)\n{\n    tcc_error(\"ppc-gen: gfunc_epilog stub\");\n}",
  "new_string": "ST_FUNC void gfunc_prolog(Sym *func_sym)\n{\n    /* Reserve PROLOG_SIZE bytes at start of function. We backfill\n     * the actual prologue instructions in gfunc_epilog once we\n     * know the final frame size. */\n    ind += PPC_PROLOG_SIZE;\n    func_sub_sp_offset = ind;  /* points just past reserved prologue */\n    loc = 0;\n    func_vc = 0;\n    /* No parameter unpacking in v1 (no params supported yet). */\n    (void)func_sym;\n}\n\nST_FUNC void gfunc_epilog(void)\n{\n    addr_t saved_ind;\n    int frame_size = ppc_frame_size();\n\n    /* Patch any pending return-jumps to land here. */\n    if (rsym) {\n        gsym(rsym);\n        rsym = 0;\n    }\n\n    /* Emit epilogue at current ind:\n     *   addi r1, r1, frame_size\n     *   lwz  r0, 8(r1)\n     *   mtlr r0\n     *   blr\n     */\n    o(0x38210000 | (frame_size & 0xffff));\n    o(0x80010008);\n    o(0x7c0803a6);\n    o(0x4e800020);\n\n    /* Backfill the reserved prologue. */\n    saved_ind = ind;\n    ind = func_sub_sp_offset - PPC_PROLOG_SIZE;\n    /* mflr r0 */\n    o(0x7c0802a6);\n    /* stw r0, 8(r1) */\n    o(0x90010008);\n    /* stwu r1, -frame_size(r1) */\n    o(0x94210000 | ((-frame_size) & 0xffff));\n    ind = saved_ind;\n}"
}

## 🤖 Claude

**🎉 First real PPC instructions!** Bytes at 0x180:

```
7c0802a6  mflr  r0
90010008  stw   r0, 8(r1)
9421ffe0  stwu  r1, -32(r1)
3860002a  li    r3, 42
38210020  addi  r1, r1, 32
80010008  lwz   r0, 8(r1)
7c0803a6  mtlr  r0
4e800020  blr
```

That's textbook Apple PPC ABI. Let me test a couple more values, then try to actually run the code.

## 🤖 Claude

Session 004 is solid. Let me commit and write up. The tccmacho.c work is the next big unblocker for end-to-end tests — that'll be session 005.

---

## 🤖 Claude


🔧 Using tool: Write
Input: {
  "file_path": "/Users/cell/claude/tcc-darwin8-ppc/docs/sessions/004-prolog-epilog-return/README.md",
  "content": "# 004 \u2014 Prologue, epilogue, return constant\n\nFirst real PPC instructions emitted from our backend. Goal: tcc\ncompiles `int main(void) { return N; }` to a correct, hand-verifiable\nsequence of 32-bit big-endian PowerPC instructions.\n\n## Arrival state\n\nAfter 003, the backend was stubbed: `tcc` could preprocess but\naborted with `gfunc_prolog stub` on first codegen. No real PPC\ninstructions emitted yet.\n\n## What was done\n\nThree real implementations in `ppc-gen.c`, plus one helper:\n\n1. **`gfunc_prolog`** \u2014 reserves `PPC_PROLOG_SIZE` (12 bytes) of\n   space at function start, records `func_sub_sp_offset` for\n   backfill. Pattern lifted from `i386-gen.c`: emit nothing yet,\n   wait for `gfunc_epilog` to know the final frame size.\n2. **`gfunc_epilog`** \u2014 emits the 4-instruction epilogue\n   (`addi/lwz/mtlr/blr`), then seeks back to\n   `func_sub_sp_offset - PROLOG_SIZE` and backfills the 3-instruction\n   prologue (`mflr/stw/stwu`). Calls `gsym(rsym)` to patch any\n   pending return-jumps (none for a single-return function, so a\n   no-op).\n3. **`load`** \u2014 handles `VT_CONST` integer immediate. Helper\n   `ppc_emit_li(gpr, v)` picks the smallest sequence:\n   - `li rD, v` (1 instr) for signed 16-bit\n   - `lis rD, hi` (1 instr) when low 16 are zero\n   - `lis rD, hi; ori rD, rD, lo` (2 instr) for full 32-bit\n4. **`ppc_frame_size()`** \u2014 Apple PPC ABI frame: 24-byte linkage area\n   + locals, rounded up to 16 bytes, minimum 32.\n\n## Test record\n\n`int main(void) { return 42; }` compiled to `.text`:\n\n```\n0x180  7c0802a6   mflr   r0          \\\n0x184  90010008   stw    r0, 8(r1)    > prologue\n0x188  9421ffe0   stwu   r1, -32(r1) /\n0x18c  3860002a   li     r3, 42       (body)\n0x190  38210020   addi   r1, r1, 32  \\\n0x194  80010008   lwz    r0, 8(r1)    \\  epilogue\n0x198  7c0803a6   mtlr   r0           /\n0x19c  4e800020   blr                /\n```\n\nHand-decoded against the PPC architecture manual (Vol 1, fixed-form\ninstruction encodings). This is exactly what gcc-4.0 emits for the\nsame input minus a couple of stylistic differences (gcc may use\n`lwz r1, 0(r1)` for back-chain-style restore instead of\n`addi r1, r1, frame_size`; both are correct).\n\nSmoke-tested seven different return values (0, 1, -1, 100, 1000,\n65536, -65535) \u2014 all encode correctly:\n\n| Value  | Bytes              | Decoded                  |\n|--------|--------------------|--------------------------|\n| 0      | `38 60 00 00`      | `li r3, 0`               |\n| 1      | `38 60 00 01`      | `li r3, 1`               |\n| -1     | `38 60 ff ff`      | `li r3, -1` (sign-ext)   |\n| 100    | `38 60 00 64`      | `li r3, 100`             |\n| 1000   | `38 60 03 e8`      | `li r3, 1000`            |\n| 65536  | `3c 60 00 01`      | `lis r3, 1`              |\n| -65535 | `3c 60 ff ff`<br>`60 63 00 01` | `lis r3, -1; ori r3, r3, 1` (= 0xFFFF0001) |\n\n## What we can't yet do\n\n- **Run the code.** `tcc -run` requires `TCC_IS_NATIVE` to be\n  defined for our (host=PPC, target=PPC) combo, which in turn pulls\n  in dependencies on `tcc_add_macos_sdkpath` and friends from\n  `tccmacho.c`. We excluded `tccmacho.c` in session 003 because it\n  hardcodes x86_64/arm64. Wired the `TCC_IS_NATIVE` macro change\n  in tcc.h but commented it out \u2014 it lights up the moment `tccmacho.c`\n  builds. Same applies to producing usable `.o` files (tcc emits\n  ELF without `tccmacho.c`, and Tiger's `ld` expects Mach-O).\n- **Anything beyond constant return.** Variables, arithmetic, calls,\n  branches \u2014 all still stub-aborts. Coming in 006+.\n\n## Decisions\n\n- **Backfill prologue pattern.** Same shape as `i386-gen.c`: skip\n  space, emit body, backfill prologue once frame size is known. Less\n  surprising than peeking ahead.\n- **PROLOG_SIZE = 12 bytes always**, even for leaf functions that\n  could skip prolog entirely. Simplifies bookkeeping; 12 bytes per\n  leaf function is negligible.\n- **Frame size minimum 32 bytes.** 24-byte linkage area + 8-byte\n  alignment slack. Above the minimum we round to 16-byte alignment\n  (Apple ABI requirement).\n- **`TCC_IS_NATIVE` for PPC commented out** with a note pointing to\n  `tccmacho.c` as the unblocker. Will reactivate in session 005.\n- **`tccrun.c` icache flush** \u2014 added `TCC_TARGET_PPC` to the\n  `__clear_cache()` invocation #if so it'll work the moment\n  `TCC_IS_NATIVE` lights up.\n\n## Exit state\n\n- Three real codegen functions (`gfunc_prolog`, `gfunc_epilog`,\n  `load` for VT_CONST integer).\n- `tcc -c hello.c` emits a 32-byte `.text` section containing\n  correct PowerPC instructions.\n- `tcc -run` blocked on `tccmacho.c` PPC support (next session).\n\n## Next session\n\nSession 005 \u2014 bring `tccmacho.c` to life on PPC. Extend the\n`#if !defined TCC_TARGET_X86_64 && !defined TCC_TARGET_ARM64`\nplatform check, audit `r_addend` usage (PPC32 uses `Rel`, not\n`Rela`), add `CPU_TYPE_POWERPC = 18`, the PPC Mach-O reloc\nenumerators (`PPC_RELOC_VANILLA` etc.), and audit anything else\nhardcoded to 64-bit. Goal: `tcc -run hello.c` returns exit code\n42 on the G3.\n\nAfter that: more codegen (locals, arithmetic, branches, function\ncalls). The cycle is short \u2014 write codegen, write a small C test,\ncompile, hand-verify or run, commit, repeat.\n"
}

## 🤖 Claude


🔧 Using tool: Bash
Input: {
  "command": "cat > /tmp/tcc-commit-msg.txt <<'COMMITEOF'\nFirst real PPC codegen: prologue/epilogue/return-constant (004)\n\nImplements gfunc_prolog, gfunc_epilog, and load() for VT_CONST\nintegers in ppc-gen.c. Tested by compiling\n  int main(void) { return N; }\nfor several values of N and hand-decoding the emitted .text:\n\n  7c0802a6  mflr   r0\n  90010008  stw    r0, 8(r1)\n  9421ffe0  stwu   r1, -32(r1)        # prologue\n  3860002a  li     r3, 42             # body (return N)\n  38210020  addi   r1, r1, 32\n  80010008  lwz    r0, 8(r1)\n  7c0803a6  mtlr   r0\n  4e800020  blr                       # epilogue\n\nApple PPC ABI: 24-byte linkage area, 16-byte stack alignment,\nminimum 32-byte frame. Prologue is reserved (PROLOG_SIZE=12 bytes)\nat gfunc_prolog time and backfilled in gfunc_epilog once frame size\nis known -- same backfill pattern as i386-gen.c.\n\nppc_emit_li picks the smallest immediate-load sequence:\n  li rD, simm           (1 instr, signed 16-bit fits)\n  lis rD, hi            (1 instr, low 16 zero)\n  lis rD, hi; ori rD,rD,lo  (2 instr, full 32-bit)\n\nVerified across return values 0, 1, -1, 100, 1000, 65536, -65535.\n\nAlso: added TCC_TARGET_PPC to tccrun.c's icache-flush #if (so\n__clear_cache fires for PPC once -run is unblocked), and prepared\nthe TCC_IS_NATIVE macro definition in tcc.h (commented out --\nwill activate in session 005 when tccmacho.c builds for PPC).\n\nEnd-to-end execution still blocked: -run requires tccmacho.c, which\nwe excluded in 003 because it hardcodes x86_64/arm64. Session 005\nunblocks that.\nCOMMITEOF\ngit commit -F /tmp/tcc-commit-msg.txt 2>&1 | tail -3 && git push 2>&1 | tail -2",
  "description": "Commit and push 004"
}

## 🤖 Claude


🔧 Using tool: Write
Input: {
  "file_path": "/Users/cell/claude/tcc-darwin8-ppc/tcc/ppc-macho-stubs.c",
  "content": "/*\n * ppc-macho-stubs.c \u2014 minimal Mach-O integration shims for PPC32.\n *\n * The real tccmacho.c is hardcoded for x86_64 / arm64 (deals with\n * Rela / 64-bit load commands / arm64 specifics) and won't compile\n * for PPC32 without significant rework. We defer that rework\n * until we need real .o file emission. Until then, this file\n * provides the minimum integration symbols that libtcc.c references\n * unconditionally on Mach-O targets, so the build links and tcc -c\n * produces (currently ELF-formatted) object files.\n *\n * Symbols provided:\n *   - tcc_add_macos_sdkpath: add Tiger-appropriate library paths.\n *   - macho_load_dll / macho_load_tbd: not supported on PPC32 yet.\n *   - macho_output_file: not supported on PPC32 yet (stubs to error).\n *\n * When real Mach-O emission lands (roadmap phase 3), this file goes\n * away and tccmacho.c picks up PPC32 alongside its x86_64/arm64.\n *\n * Copyright (c) 2026 the tcc-darwin8-ppc contributors. MIT.\n */\n\n#include \"tcc.h\"\n\nST_FUNC void tcc_add_macos_sdkpath(TCCState *s)\n{\n    /* Tiger does not have xcode-select or .sdk bundles. The system\n     * libraries live in /usr/lib and headers in /usr/include \u2014 both\n     * are flat, no SDK indirection. The configure script already\n     * configures /usr/include as the system include dir; here we\n     * just add /usr/lib as a library search path. */\n    tcc_add_library_path(s, \"/usr/lib\");\n}\n\nST_FUNC int macho_load_dll(TCCState *s1, int fd, const char *filename, int lev)\n{\n    (void)fd; (void)lev;\n    tcc_error_noabort(\"ppc-macho-stubs: dynamic library loading \"\n                      \"not yet supported (file: %s)\", filename);\n    return -1;\n}\n\nST_FUNC int macho_load_tbd(TCCState *s1, int fd, const char *filename, int lev)\n{\n    (void)fd; (void)lev;\n    tcc_error_noabort(\"ppc-macho-stubs: .tbd library loading \"\n                      \"not yet supported (file: %s)\", filename);\n    return -1;\n}\n\nST_FUNC int macho_output_file(TCCState *s1, const char *filename)\n{\n    (void)s1;\n    tcc_error_noabort(\"ppc-macho-stubs: Mach-O output not yet \"\n                      \"implemented (would write %s)\", filename);\n    return -1;\n}\n"
}

Input: {
  "replace_all": false,
  "file_path": "/Users/cell/claude/tcc-darwin8-ppc/tcc/tccrun.c",
  "old_string": "    tcc_add_symbol(s1, \"__rt_exit\", rt_exit);\n    s1->run_main = \"_runmain\", top_sym = \"main\";\n    if (s1->elf_entryname)\n        s1->run_main = top_sym = s1->elf_entryname;\n    tcc_add_support(s1, \"runmain.o\");",
  "new_string": "    tcc_add_symbol(s1, \"__rt_exit\", rt_exit);\n    s1->run_main = \"_runmain\", top_sym = \"main\";\n    if (s1->elf_entryname)\n        s1->run_main = top_sym = s1->elf_entryname;\n#ifdef TCC_TARGET_PPC\n    /* PPC bootstrap: tccmacho.c can't yet load gcc-built runmain.o\n     * (it's Mach-O .o, our loader is ELF only). And our codegen\n     * isn't yet rich enough to compile lib/runmain.c with tcc itself.\n     * Until either of those lands, call main() directly. The\n     * downside: no atexit, no init/fini arrays, no on_exit(). Fine\n     * for early hello-world tests; revisit when we land Mach-O .o\n     * loading or grow codegen enough to compile runmain.c. */\n    s1->run_main = top_sym;\n#else\n    tcc_add_support(s1, \"runmain.o\");\n#endif"
}

## 🤖 Claude

# 🎉 First end-to-end execution on the G3!

`tcc -run` compiled our PPC code and ran it. `exit=42`. That's tcc → PPC bytes → mmap'd memory → executed → returned 42 → propagated as exit code. Let me verify with more values.

---

## 🤖 Claude


🔧 Using tool: Bash
Input: {
  "command": "ssh imacg3 'cd ~/tcc-darwin8-ppc/tcc && for v in 0 1 7 100 -1 65536 -65535 12345; do\n  echo \"int main(void){return $v;}\" > /tmp/t.c\n  ./tcc -B. -run /tmp/t.c; printf \"  return %s -> exit %d\\n\" \"$v\" \"$?\"\ndone'",
  "description": "Smoke test multiple return values"
}


---

## 🤖 Claude


🔧 Using tool: Bash
Input: {
  "command": "ssh imacg3 'cd ~/tcc-darwin8-ppc/tcc && echo \"int main(void){return 1+2;}\" > /tmp/t.c && ./tcc -B. -run /tmp/t.c 2>&1; echo exit=$?'",
  "description": "Test arithmetic (should hit stub)"
}


---

## 🤖 Claude

`return 1+2;` → exit 3 — tcc's parser folds constants before codegen, so this works without us implementing `gen_opi`. Real arithmetic on variables will need gen_opi, that's still ahead.

Let me close out 005 properly.

---

## 🤖 Claude


🔧 Using tool: Write
Input: {
  "file_path": "/Users/cell/claude/tcc-darwin8-ppc/docs/sessions/005-macho-stubs/README.md",
  "content": "# 005 \u2014 Mach-O integration stubs and first end-to-end execution\n\n\ud83c\udf89 **Milestone:** `tcc -run` executes PPC code generated by our\nbackend on a real G3, returning the right exit code. First true\nend-to-end win.\n\n## Arrival state\n\nAfter 004, tcc emitted correct PPC instructions but couldn't run\nthem. `tcc -run` was gated on `TCC_IS_NATIVE`, which in turn\nrequired `tccmacho.c` (excluded since 003 because it's hardcoded\nfor x86_64/arm64).\n\n## What was done\n\n1. **`tcc/ppc-macho-stubs.c` added.** Minimal integration shims\n   for the four Mach-O symbols `libtcc.c` references unconditionally:\n   - `tcc_add_macos_sdkpath` \u2014 adds `/usr/lib` (Tiger has no\n     `xcode-select`, no `.sdk` bundles).\n   - `macho_load_dll`, `macho_load_tbd` \u2014 error stubs (no dynamic\n     library loading yet).\n   - `macho_output_file` \u2014 error stub (no Mach-O output yet).\n   - `macho_tbd_soname` \u2014 returns NULL.\n   Wired into the build via `Makefile` (replaces `tccmacho.c` in\n   `ppc-osx_FILES`).\n\n2. **`TCC_IS_NATIVE` enabled for PPC native** (uncommented the\n   block in `tcc.h` from 004).\n\n3. **`tccrun.c` runmain.o bypass for PPC.** The unmodified\n   tccrun.c calls `tcc_add_support(s1, \"runmain.o\")` to load a\n   wrapper that handles atexit/init_array/main-call sequencing.\n   Loading `runmain.o` requires either:\n   - Compiling `lib/runmain.c` with our tcc \u2014 blocked, our codegen\n     can't yet handle init_array / atexit / extern symbols.\n   - Loading a gcc-compiled `runmain.o` \u2014 blocked, our loader is\n     ELF-only (Mach-O .o reading isn't implemented).\n   So under `#ifdef TCC_TARGET_PPC` we skip the support load and\n   call `main()` directly. Trade-off: no atexit, no static\n   ctors/dtors. Acceptable for the early demo phase. Reverts when\n   either of the unblockers above lands.\n\n## Test record\n\n```\n$ ./tcc -B. -run /tmp/r42.c\nexit=42\n\n$ for v in 0 1 7 100 -1 65536 -65535 12345; do\n    echo \"int main(void){return $v;}\" > /tmp/t.c\n    ./tcc -B. -run /tmp/t.c; printf \"  return %s -> exit %d\\n\" \"$v\" \"$?\"\n  done\n  return 0 -> exit 0\n  return 1 -> exit 1\n  return 7 -> exit 7\n  return 100 -> exit 100\n  return -1 -> exit 255       (Unix 8-bit truncation: -1 & 0xff = 255)\n  return 65536 -> exit 0      (65536 & 0xff = 0)\n  return -65535 -> exit 1     (-65535 = 0xffff0001 & 0xff = 1)\n  return 12345 -> exit 57     (12345 & 0xff = 57)\n```\n\nAll values correct after accounting for the Unix exit-code\ntruncation to low 8 bits.\n\nBonus: `int main(void) { return 1+2; }` returns 3. That's\nconstant-folding in tcc's parser working without our codegen \u2014 `1+2`\nbecomes literal `3` before reaching `load()`. So basic compile-time\narithmetic \"works\" already. Real runtime arithmetic on variables\nneeds `gen_opi`, which is still a stub.\n\n## Limitations introduced (technical debt)\n\n- **No atexit / no static init/fini arrays** in -run mode. From\n  bypassing `runmain.o`. Will be fixed once we can either compile\n  runmain.c with tcc or load Mach-O .o files.\n- **No dynamic library loading** (printf, malloc, etc.). `macho_load_dll`\n  is a stub. So programs that need libc are not yet runnable. We\n  unblock this when tccmacho.c handles PPC (or we add a minimal\n  Mach-O .dylib reader path).\n- **No Mach-O output.** `tcc -c` emits ELF-format .o files that\n  Tiger's `ld` can't link. Same unblock plan.\n\n## Decisions\n\n- **`ppc-macho-stubs.c` is a temporary scaffold,** not a permanent\n  fixture. When tccmacho.c gains real PPC support (roadmap phase 3,\n  estimated session ~020), this file is deleted and tccmacho.c\n  picks up the slack.\n- **Bypass under `#ifdef TCC_TARGET_PPC`** in tccrun.c, with a\n  comment explaining the conditions for removing it. This is a\n  surgical scope: doesn't affect any other target.\n\n## Exit state\n\nThe G3 can compile and execute `int main(void) { return N; }` for\narbitrary N via `tcc -B. -run /tmp/file.c`. End-to-end pipeline\nproven. We can iterate fast on codegen now: write a stub\nimplementation, test it on the G3, commit.\n\n## Next session\n\nSession 006 \u2014 start growing real codegen so we can compile useful\nprograms. Order of attack (from cheapest signal to most complex):\n\n1. **Local variables and the assignment operator** (`store`, plus\n   load for `VT_LOCAL`/`VT_LVAL`). Lets `int x = 7; return x;`\n   work.\n2. **Integer arithmetic** (`gen_opi` for + - * / and bitwise ops).\n   Lets `int x = 1; return x + 2;` work.\n3. **Branches** (`gjmp`, `gjmp_cond`, `gsym_addr`). Lets `if`,\n   `while`, `for` work.\n4. **Function calls** \u2014 Apple PPC ABI calling convention.\n\nEach step gets a small C test under `tests/`, compiled and run via\n`tcc -B. -run`, exit code checked.\n"
}

Input: {
  "file_path": "/Users/cell/claude/tcc-darwin8-ppc/README.md",
  "content": "# tcc-darwin8-ppc\n\nA Mac OS X 10.4 Tiger / PowerPC backend for [tcc](https://repo.or.cz/tinycc.git),\nthe Tiny C Compiler.\n\n**Status: very early but moving.** TCC has never had a PowerPC\nbackend; we're writing one from scratch. As of session 005, tcc\ncompiles `int main(void) { return N; }` to correct PowerPC\ninstructions and executes them on a real G3 via `tcc -run`. Real\ncodegen (variables, arithmetic, calls) is the next ramp.\n\nThe first major milestone is a `/opt`-installable G3 tarball that\nself-hosts on Tiger.\n\n## Status\n\n| | |\n|---|---|\n| \u2705 | Source base chosen: [mob branch @ b39da9f6](tcc/.UPSTREAM.md) |\n| \u2705 | Architecture & licensing surveyed ([000](docs/sessions/000-source-survey/README.md), [001](docs/sessions/001-fork-comparison/README.md)) |\n| \u2705 | Baseline builds verified on uranium (host) and imacg3 (target) ([002](docs/sessions/002-baseline-build/README.md)) |\n| \u2705 | Backend scaffold \u2014 `tcc` builds, runs, preprocesses ([003](docs/sessions/003-backend-scaffold/README.md)) |\n| \u2705 | First real PPC instructions emitted, hand-verified ([004](docs/sessions/004-prolog-epilog-return/README.md)) |\n| \u2705 | **`tcc -run` executes PPC code on G3, exit codes propagate** ([005](docs/sessions/005-macho-stubs/README.md)) |\n| \u23f3 | Local variables, assignment, arithmetic |\n| \u23f3 | Branches (`if`, `while`, `for`) |\n| \u23f3 | Function calls (Apple PPC ABI) |\n| \u23f3 | Floating point |\n| \u23f3 | `tccmacho.c` PPC support (real .o output, dylib loading) |\n| \u274c | Self-host bootstrap on Tiger |\n| \u274c | G3 tarball |\n\n[Roadmap](docs/roadmap.md) \u2022 [Sessions](docs/sessions/)\n\n## What this project is\n\nTCC ships with backends for i386, x86_64, ARM, ARM64, C67, and\nRISC-V64. **It has never had a PowerPC backend** in any release\n(Bellard's, the 0.9.27 community release, or the active mob branch).\nThis project adds one, targeting 32-bit PowerPC on Mac OS X 10.4\nTiger Mach-O.\n\nIn addition to the codegen, this requires extending TCC's\n`tccmacho.c` (currently hardcoded for x86_64 and arm64) to emit\n32-bit PPC Mach-O object files and dyld-compatible executables.\n\n## Why\n\n- TCC compiles fast, in a place where everything else is slow.\n  GCC takes minutes on a G3; even a quarter-functional TCC could\n  meaningfully speed up small builds on the platform.\n- Self-hosting a from-scratch compiler backend on a 22-year-old\n  machine is fun.\n- PowerPC ISA is genuinely pleasant \u2014 a fixed-size 32-bit\n  instruction set with a clean RISC dispatch model.\n\n## Building (G3, Tiger)\n\n```sh\nssh imacg3\ngit clone <this-repo> ~/tcc-darwin8-ppc\ncd ~/tcc-darwin8-ppc/tcc\n./configure --config-semlock=no\n/opt/make-4.3/bin/make           # system make is GNU 3.80, lacks $(or)\n./tcc -B. -run hello.c           # works for \"int main(void) { return N; }\"\n```\n\nNotes:\n- `--config-semlock=no` is required: tcc.h's `__APPLE__` branch\n  hardcodes Grand Central Dispatch (10.6+); Tiger has POSIX\n  semaphores only. The semlock feature isn't needed for our use.\n- System `make` is GNU 3.80 (2002), which doesn't support `$(or\n  ...)`. Use `/opt/make-4.3/bin/make` (preinstalled on the\n  PowerPC fleet via `tiger.sh`).\n\n## Building (uranium, host build for sanity check)\n\n```sh\ncd tcc\n./configure\nmake -j8        # builds an arm64-osx tcc, has nothing to do with PPC\n                # but confirms our patches don't break upstream targets\n```\n\n## Layout\n\n```\ntcc/                     - upstream mob source, modified in place. No\n                           patch-set workflow. tcc/.UPSTREAM.md notes\n                           the snapshot commit.\n  ppc-gen.c              - PPC32 code generator (we wrote this).\n  ppc-link.c             - PPC32 ELF relocation handling (we wrote).\n  ppc-macho-stubs.c      - integration shims while tccmacho.c lacks\n                           PPC support; deleted when phase 3 lands.\ndocs/\n  roadmap.md             - phases and per-session breakdown.\n  sessions/NNN-<slug>/   - per-session narrative + findings.\n```\n\n## Sister projects\n\n(Currently private. Will cross-link once they're public.)\n\n## License\n\nProject code is MIT-licensed. The `tcc/` subdirectory inherits\nupstream TCC licensing \u2014 LGPL 2.1 with most files MIT-relicensed\nper [`tcc/RELICENSING`](tcc/RELICENSING). A handful of files\n(notably `arm-gen.c`) remain LGPL-only; we don't copy from those.\n\nSee [`tcc/COPYING`](tcc/COPYING) and\n[`tcc/RELICENSING`](tcc/RELICENSING) for upstream details.\n"
}

Verified for N in {0, 1, 7, 100, -1, 65536, -65535,\n12345}; all values produce the correct (Unix 8-bit truncated) exit.\n\nThree changes get us there:\n\n1. ppc-macho-stubs.c (new file) \u2014 minimal integration shims for the\n   four Mach-O symbols libtcc.c references unconditionally on Mach-O\n   targets: tcc_add_macos_sdkpath, macho_load_dll, macho_load_tbd,\n   macho_output_file, macho_tbd_soname. These let the build link with\n   TCC_IS_NATIVE on. Real tccmacho.c PPC support is roadmap phase 3.\n\n2. tcc.h \u2014 uncommented the TCC_IS_NATIVE macro for ppc + Darwin so\n   tccrun.c is compiled in.\n\n3. tccrun.c \u2014 bypass tcc_add_support(\"runmain.o\") under TCC_TARGET_PPC.\n   Loading runmain.o needs either a working tcc that can compile\n   lib/runmain.c (blocked: codegen too thin) or a Mach-O .o reader\n   (blocked: tccmacho.c excluded). ## 🤖 Claude

I've reached a good pausing point. We have **end-to-end execution working on the G3** — significant unblocking milestone. The foundation is laid; future sessions iterate on codegen.

## Summary of unsupervised work

**5 commits pushed** to [tcc-darwin8-ppc](https://github.com/cellularmitosis/tcc-darwin8-ppc/commits/main):

| Session | What landed |
|---|---|
| [002](https://github.com/cellularmitosis/tcc-darwin8-ppc/commit/2924827) | Pulled mob in-tree at [tcc/](https://github.com/cellularmitosis/tcc-darwin8-ppc/tree/main/tcc), wrote [roadmap](https://github.com/cellularmitosis/tcc-darwin8-ppc/blob/main/docs/roadmap.md), baseline both hosts |
| [003](https://github.com/cellularmitosis/tcc-darwin8-ppc/commit/07f133c) | Backend scaffold — tcc builds on G3, preprocesses, aborts on first stub |
| [004](https://github.com/cellularmitosis/tcc-darwin8-ppc/commit/d9870b7) | Real prologue/epilogue/load — tcc emits correct PPC instructions for `return N` |
| [005](https://github.com/cellularmitosis/tcc-darwin8-ppc/commit/4ecc43d) | macho stubs + runmain bypass — **`tcc -run` executes on G3** |

**5 blockers** diagnosed and fixed along the way:
1. tcc.h's target-fallback chain didn't know PPC (silently selected i386)
2. Tiger's GNU make 3.80 lacks `$(or ...)` — use `/opt/make-4.3/bin/make`
3. `tccmacho.c` hardcodes x86_64/arm64 — replaced with `ppc-macho-stubs.c` for now
4. `tcc_error()` macro requires `USING_GLOBALS` — link.c uses `tcc_error_noabort()`
5. `func_bound_add_epilog` referenced unconditionally in tccgen.c despite gated declaration

**Test record** ([details in 005/README.md](docs/sessions/005-macho-stubs/README.md)):

```
$ ./tcc -B. -run /tmp/r42.c
exit=42

$ for v in 0 1 7 100 -1 65536 -65535 12345; do
    echo "int main(void){return $v;}" > /tmp/t.c
    ./tcc -B. -run /tmp/t.c; echo "$v -> $?"
  done
0 -> 0     1 -> 1     7 -> 7     100 -> 100
-1 -> 255  65536 -> 0  -65535 -> 1  12345 -> 57
```

All correct (Unix 8-bit exit truncation accounted for).

**Where to pick up:** session 006 — local variables and assignment (`store`, `load` for `VT_LOCAL`), then arithmetic, branches, function calls. Each gets a small C test compiled and run via `tcc -B. -run` on imacg3. Roadmap phases 2-6 detail the rest. The G3 tarball goal is realistic but still a substantial codegen project ahead.

I stopped here rather than push deeper because (a) this is a clean milestone, (b) I'd burned a lot of context, and (c) the user now has a working PPC tcc on the G3 they can poke at while deciding next steps. Not a hard blocker — just a natural breath point.

  "file_path": "/Users/cell/claude/tcc-darwin8-ppc/scripts/build-tiger.sh",
  "content": "#!/bin/sh\n# build-tiger.sh \u2014 build tcc on Tiger PowerPC.\n#\n# Wraps the configure + make sequence with the Tiger-specific bits:\n#   - --config-semlock=no:  tcc.h's __APPLE__ branch hardcodes\n#                            Grand Central Dispatch (10.6+); Tiger\n#                            has POSIX semaphores only.\n#   - /opt/make-4.3/bin/make: system make is GNU 3.80, lacks $(or).\n#                              Auto-installs via tiger.sh if missing.\n#\n# Run from the repo root or from tcc/.\n\nset -e\n\ncd \"$(dirname \"$0\")/../tcc\"\n\nMAKE=/opt/make-4.3/bin/make\nif [ ! -x \"$MAKE\" ]; then\n    if command -v tiger.sh >/dev/null 2>&1; then\n        echo \"make-4.3 not found; installing via tiger.sh...\"\n        tiger.sh make-4.3\n    else\n        echo \"ERROR: $MAKE not found and tiger.sh is unavailable.\"\n        echo \"Install GNU make >= 3.81 manually and re-run with MAKE=...\"\n        exit 1\n    fi\nfi\n\nif [ ! -f config.mak ] || [ \"$1\" = \"configure\" ]; then\n    ./configure --config-semlock=no\n    shift 2>/dev/null || :\nfi\n\nexec \"$MAKE\" \"$@\"\n"
}

## 🤖 Claude

GCC reference: FP is r30 (or r31), locals at offsets from FP. Our existing register pool excludes r31, so we can use it as FP without conflict. Let me implement: prologue saves r31, epilogue restores; `store`/`load` handle `VT_LOCAL` via `r31+offset`.

---

  "description": "Start session 006"
}

## 🤖 Claude


🔧 Using tool: Edit
Input: {
  "replace_all": false,
  "file_path": "/Users/cell/claude/tcc-darwin8-ppc/tcc/ppc-gen.c",
  "old_string": "/* Apple PPC ABI:\n *   - 24-byte linkage area at top of caller's frame (back-chain,\n *     saved CR, saved LR, three reserved words).\n *   - Stack alignment is 16 bytes.\n *   - Outgoing param area sits above linkage; we don't yet allocate\n *     extra space for it (no calls in v1 codegen).\n *\n * PROLOG_SIZE = 3 instructions = 12 bytes:\n *   mflr r0\n *   stw  r0, 8(r1)\n *   stwu r1, -frame_size(r1)\n */\n#define PPC_PROLOG_SIZE 12\n#define PPC_LINKAGE_SIZE 24\n#define PPC_STACK_ALIGN 16\n\nstatic int ppc_frame_size(void)\n{\n    /* loc is non-positive (locals grow downward). Frame holds\n     * linkage + locals, rounded up to 16. Minimum 32 keeps things\n     * sane for trivial leaf functions. */\n    int n = PPC_LINKAGE_SIZE + (-loc);\n    n = (n + PPC_STACK_ALIGN - 1) & -PPC_STACK_ALIGN;\n    if (n < 32) n = 32;\n    return n;\n}",
  "new_string": "/* Apple PPC ABI frame layout. After our prologue:\n *\n *   high addr (caller's frame)\n *   +----+ <-- OLD_SP, our r31 (frame pointer / FP)\n *   | r31 save (4 bytes)        <-- at r31-4 (i.e., loc=-4 reserved)\n *   | user locals (loc=-8 down) <-- addressed via offset(r31)\n *   |    ...\n *   | 24-byte linkage area\n *   +----+ <-- NEW_SP, our r1\n *\n * r31 is a callee-saved register in the Apple PPC ABI; we save it\n * to the top of our own frame and use it as a stable frame pointer\n * for the duration of the function. Locals are addressed at\n * negative offsets from r31 (matching tcc's `loc` convention from\n * i386-gen.c).\n *\n * Prologue (5 instructions = 20 bytes):\n *   mflr r0\n *   stw  r0, 8(r1)              ; save LR in caller's linkage area\n *   stwu r1, -frame_size(r1)    ; allocate frame, set back-chain\n *   stw  r31, frame_size-4(r1)  ; save old r31 at our frame top\n *   addi r31, r1, frame_size    ; FP = OLD_SP\n *\n * Epilogue (5 instructions = 20 bytes):\n *   lwz  r31, frame_size-4(r1)  ; restore r31 (must precede addi)\n *   addi r1, r1, frame_size     ; restore SP\n *   lwz  r0, 8(r1)              ; reload LR from caller's linkage\n *   mtlr r0\n *   blr\n */\n#define PPC_PROLOG_SIZE 20\n#define PPC_LINKAGE_SIZE 24\n#define PPC_STACK_ALIGN 16\n#define PPC_FP_REG 31\n\nstatic int ppc_frame_size(void)\n{\n    /* loc is non-positive (locals grow downward). loc=-4 is reserved\n     * for the FP-save slot at the top of the frame. User locals\n     * start at loc=-8. Frame holds linkage + (-loc) bytes of saved-\n     * FP-plus-locals, rounded up to 16. Minimum 32. */\n    int n = PPC_LINKAGE_SIZE + (-loc);\n    n = (n + PPC_STACK_ALIGN - 1) & -PPC_STACK_ALIGN;\n    if (n < 32) n = 32;\n    return n;\n}"
}

## 🤖 Claude


🔧 Using tool: Edit
Input: {
  "replace_all": false,
  "file_path": "/Users/cell/claude/tcc-darwin8-ppc/tcc/ppc-gen.c",
  "old_string": "ST_FUNC void load(int r, SValue *sv)\n{\n    int v = sv->r & VT_VALMASK;\n    int gpr;\n\n    /* v1: only handle integer constant immediate. Everything else\n     * stubs to a clear error message. */\n    if (v == VT_CONST && !(sv->r & VT_LVAL) && !(sv->r & VT_SYM)) {\n        if (IS_FREG(r)) {\n            tcc_error(\"ppc-gen: load FP constant not yet implemented\");\n        }\n        gpr = TREG_TO_GPR(r);\n        ppc_emit_li(gpr, (int32_t)sv->c.i);\n        return;\n    }\n\n    tcc_error(\"ppc-gen: load stub (r=%d sv->r=0x%x type=0x%x val=0x%llx)\",\n              r, sv->r, sv->type.t, (long long)sv->c.i);\n}",
  "new_string": "ST_FUNC void load(int r, SValue *sv)\n{\n    int v = sv->r & VT_VALMASK;\n    int gpr;\n    int offset;\n\n    /* Integer constant immediate. */\n    if (v == VT_CONST && !(sv->r & VT_LVAL) && !(sv->r & VT_SYM)) {\n        if (IS_FREG(r)) {\n            tcc_error(\"ppc-gen: load FP constant not yet implemented\");\n        }\n        gpr = TREG_TO_GPR(r);\n        ppc_emit_li(gpr, (int32_t)sv->c.i);\n        return;\n    }\n\n    /* Local variable, address taken (rvalue = its address). */\n    if (v == VT_LOCAL && !(sv->r & VT_LVAL)) {\n        if (IS_FREG(r)) tcc_error(\"ppc-gen: address-of into FP register?\");\n        gpr = TREG_TO_GPR(r);\n        offset = (int)sv->c.i;\n        /* addi rD, r31, offset */\n        o(0x38000000 | (gpr << 21) | (PPC_FP_REG << 16) | (offset & 0xffff));\n        return;\n    }\n\n    /* Local variable, lvalue read (load from memory at FP+offset). */\n    if (v == VT_LOCAL && (sv->r & VT_LVAL)) {\n        int bt = sv->type.t & VT_BTYPE;\n        offset = (int)sv->c.i;\n        if (IS_FREG(r)) {\n            tcc_error(\"ppc-gen: FP local load not yet implemented\");\n        }\n        gpr = TREG_TO_GPR(r);\n        switch (bt) {\n        case VT_BYTE:\n            /* lbz rD, d(rA) \u2014 opcode 34. Sign-extend with extsb if signed. */\n            o(0x88000000 | (gpr << 21) | (PPC_FP_REG << 16) | (offset & 0xffff));\n            if (!(sv->type.t & VT_UNSIGNED))\n                o(0x7c000774 | (gpr << 21) | (gpr << 16));  /* extsb rA, rS */\n            return;\n        case VT_SHORT:\n            if (sv->type.t & VT_UNSIGNED) {\n                /* lhz \u2014 opcode 40 */\n                o(0xa0000000 | (gpr << 21) | (PPC_FP_REG << 16) | (offset & 0xffff));\n            } else {\n                /* lha \u2014 opcode 42 (sign-extending) */\n                o(0xa8000000 | (gpr << 21) | (PPC_FP_REG << 16) | (offset & 0xffff));\n            }\n            return;\n        case VT_INT:\n        case VT_PTR:\n        case VT_FUNC:\n            /* lwz rD, d(rA) \u2014 opcode 32 = 0x80000000 */\n            o(0x80000000 | (gpr << 21) | (PPC_FP_REG << 16) | (offset & 0xffff));\n            return;\n        default:\n            tcc_error(\"ppc-gen: load VT_LOCAL of basic type 0x%x not yet supported\", bt);\n        }\n    }\n\n    tcc_error(\"ppc-gen: load stub (r=%d sv->r=0x%x type=0x%x val=0x%llx)\",\n              r, sv->r, sv->type.t, (long long)sv->c.i);\n}"
}

  "old_string": "ST_FUNC void store(int r, SValue *sv)\n{\n    tcc_error(\"ppc-gen: store stub (r=%d)\", r);\n}",
  "new_string": "ST_FUNC void store(int r, SValue *sv)\n{\n    int v = sv->r & VT_VALMASK;\n    int gpr;\n    int offset;\n    int bt = sv->type.t & VT_BTYPE;\n\n    if (v == VT_LOCAL) {\n        offset = (int)sv->c.i;\n        if (IS_FREG(r)) {\n            tcc_error(\"ppc-gen: store FP local not yet implemented\");\n        }\n        gpr = TREG_TO_GPR(r);\n        switch (bt) {\n        case VT_BYTE:\n            /* stb rS, d(rA) \u2014 opcode 38 */\n            o(0x98000000 | (gpr << 21) | (PPC_FP_REG << 16) | (offset & 0xffff));\n            return;\n        case VT_SHORT:\n            /* sth rS, d(rA) \u2014 opcode 44 */\n            o(0xb0000000 | (gpr << 21) | (PPC_FP_REG << 16) | (offset & 0xffff));\n            return;\n        case VT_INT:\n        case VT_PTR:\n        case VT_FUNC:\n            /* stw rS, d(rA) \u2014 opcode 36 = 0x90000000 */\n            o(0x90000000 | (gpr << 21) | (PPC_FP_REG << 16) | (offset & 0xffff));\n            return;\n        default:\n            tcc_error(\"ppc-gen: store VT_LOCAL of bt 0x%x not yet supported\", bt);\n        }\n    }\n\n    tcc_error(\"ppc-gen: store stub (r=%d sv->r=0x%x bt=0x%x)\", r, sv->r, bt);\n}"

  "replace_all": false,
  "file_path": "/Users/cell/claude/tcc-darwin8-ppc/tcc/ppc-gen.c",
  "old_string": "ST_FUNC void gfunc_prolog(Sym *func_sym)\n{\n    /* Reserve PROLOG_SIZE bytes at start of function. We backfill\n     * the actual prologue instructions in gfunc_epilog once we\n     * know the final frame size. */\n    ind += PPC_PROLOG_SIZE;\n    func_sub_sp_offset = ind;  /* points just past reserved prologue */\n    loc = 0;\n    func_vc = 0;\n    /* No parameter unpacking in v1 (no params supported yet). */\n    (void)func_sym;\n}\n\nST_FUNC void gfunc_epilog(void)\n{\n    addr_t saved_ind;\n    int frame_size = ppc_frame_size();\n\n    /* Patch any pending return-jumps to land here. */\n    if (rsym) {\n        gsym(rsym);\n        rsym = 0;\n    }\n\n    /* Emit epilogue at current ind:\n     *   addi r1, r1, frame_size\n     *   lwz  r0, 8(r1)\n     *   mtlr r0\n     *   blr\n     */\n    o(0x38210000 | (frame_size & 0xffff));\n    o(0x80010008);\n    o(0x7c0803a6);\n    o(0x4e800020);\n\n    /* Backfill the reserved prologue. */\n    saved_ind = ind;\n    ind = func_sub_sp_offset - PPC_PROLOG_SIZE;\n    /* mflr r0 */\n    o(0x7c0802a6);\n    /* stw r0, 8(r1) */\n    o(0x90010008);\n    /* stwu r1, -frame_size(r1) */\n    o(0x94210000 | ((-frame_size) & 0xffff));\n    ind = saved_ind;\n}",
  "new_string": "ST_FUNC void gfunc_prolog(Sym *func_sym)\n{\n    /* Reserve PROLOG_SIZE bytes for backfill in gfunc_epilog. */\n    ind += PPC_PROLOG_SIZE;\n    func_sub_sp_offset = ind;\n    /* loc=-4 reserves the FP-save slot at r31-4. User locals begin\n     * at loc=-8 after tcc's first allocation. */\n    loc = -4;\n    func_vc = 0;\n    /* No parameter unpacking in v1 (no params supported yet). */\n    (void)func_sym;\n}\n\nST_FUNC void gfunc_epilog(void)\n{\n    addr_t saved_ind;\n    int frame_size = ppc_frame_size();\n    int fp_save_off = frame_size - 4;  /* offset of saved-r31 slot from new SP */\n\n    /* Patch any pending return-jumps to land here. */\n    if (rsym) {\n        gsym(rsym);\n        rsym = 0;\n    }\n\n    /* Epilogue:\n     *   lwz  r31, fp_save_off(r1)\n     *   addi r1, r1, frame_size\n     *   lwz  r0, 8(r1)\n     *   mtlr r0\n     *   blr\n     */\n    o(0x80000000 | (PPC_FP_REG << 21) | (1 << 16) | (fp_save_off & 0xffff));\n    o(0x38210000 | (frame_size & 0xffff));\n    o(0x80010008);\n    o(0x7c0803a6);\n    o(0x4e800020);\n\n    /* Backfill the reserved prologue. */\n    saved_ind = ind;\n    ind = func_sub_sp_offset - PPC_PROLOG_SIZE;\n    /* mflr r0 */\n    o(0x7c0802a6);\n    /* stw r0, 8(r1) */\n    o(0x90010008);\n    /* stwu r1, -frame_size(r1) */\n    o(0x94210000 | ((-frame_size) & 0xffff));\n    /* stw r31, fp_save_off(r1) */\n    o(0x90000000 | (PPC_FP_REG << 21) | (1 << 16) | (fp_save_off & 0xffff));\n    /* addi r31, r1, frame_size */\n    o(0x38000000 | (PPC_FP_REG << 21) | (1 << 16) | (frame_size & 0xffff));\n    ind = saved_ind;\n}"
}

## 🤖 Claude


🔧 Using tool: Edit
Input: {
  "replace_all": false,
  "file_path": "/Users/cell/claude/tcc-darwin8-ppc/tcc/ppc-gen.c",
  "old_string": "ST_FUNC void gen_opi(int op)\n{\n    tcc_error(\"ppc-gen: gen_opi stub (op=0x%x)\", op);\n}",
  "new_string": "/* Two-register integer ops. Result goes into vtop[-1]'s slot;\n * vtop[0] is consumed.\n *\n * PPC has two main encoding shapes for these:\n *   XO-form arithmetic (add/sub/mul):  rD at 21..25, rA at 16..20, rB at 11..15\n *   X-form logical / shift:            rS at 21..25, rA(=dst) at 16..20, rB at 11..15\n */\nST_FUNC void gen_opi(int op)\n{\n    int ra_slot, rb_slot, ra_gpr, rb_gpr;\n\n    /* Comparisons set VT_CMP; we don't yet implement them.\n     * Branches/conditionals come in a later session. */\n    if (op >= TOK_ULT && op <= TOK_GT)\n        tcc_error(\"ppc-gen: integer comparisons not yet implemented (op=0x%x)\", op);\n\n    gv2(RC_INT, RC_INT);\n    ra_slot = vtop[-1].r;\n    rb_slot = vtop[0].r;\n    ra_gpr = TREG_TO_GPR(ra_slot);\n    rb_gpr = TREG_TO_GPR(rb_slot);\n\n    switch (op) {\n    case '+':\n        /* add rD, rA, rB */\n        o(0x7c000214 | (ra_gpr << 21) | (ra_gpr << 16) | (rb_gpr << 11));\n        break;\n    case '-':\n        /* subf rD, rA, rB  computes rD = rB - rA, so swap. */\n        o(0x7c000050 | (ra_gpr << 21) | (rb_gpr << 16) | (ra_gpr << 11));\n        break;\n    case '*':\n        /* mullw rD, rA, rB */\n        o(0x7c0001d6 | (ra_gpr << 21) | (ra_gpr << 16) | (rb_gpr << 11));\n        break;\n    case '&':\n        /* and rA, rS, rB */\n        o(0x7c000038 | (ra_gpr << 21) | (ra_gpr << 16) | (rb_gpr << 11));\n        break;\n    case '|':\n        /* or rA, rS, rB */\n        o(0x7c000378 | (ra_gpr << 21) | (ra_gpr << 16) | (rb_gpr << 11));\n        break;\n    case '^':\n        /* xor rA, rS, rB */\n        o(0x7c000278 | (ra_gpr << 21) | (ra_gpr << 16) | (rb_gpr << 11));\n        break;\n    case TOK_SHL:\n        /* slw rA, rS, rB */\n        o(0x7c000030 | (ra_gpr << 21) | (ra_gpr << 16) | (rb_gpr << 11));\n        break;\n    case TOK_SHR:\n        /* srw rA, rS, rB (logical) */\n        o(0x7c000430 | (ra_gpr << 21) | (ra_gpr << 16) | (rb_gpr << 11));\n        break;\n    case TOK_SAR:\n        /* sraw rA, rS, rB (arithmetic) */\n        o(0x7c000630 | (ra_gpr << 21) | (ra_gpr << 16) | (rb_gpr << 11));\n        break;\n    case '/':\n        /* divw rD, rA, rB (signed) */\n        o(0x7c0003d6 | (ra_gpr << 21) | (ra_gpr << 16) | (rb_gpr << 11));\n        break;\n    case TOK_UDIV:\n        /* divwu rD, rA, rB (unsigned) */\n        o(0x7c000396 | (ra_gpr << 21) | (ra_gpr << 16) | (rb_gpr << 11));\n        break;\n    case '%':\n    case TOK_UMOD:\n        /* PPC has no modulo instruction; compute as a - (a/b)*b.\n         * Use a temp register? For now, error so we notice. */\n        tcc_error(\"ppc-gen: modulo not yet implemented\");\n        break;\n    default:\n        tcc_error(\"ppc-gen: gen_opi op 0x%x not yet supported\", op);\n    }\n    vtop--;\n}"
}

## 🤖 Claude


🔧 Using tool: Edit
Input: {
  "replace_all": false,
  "file_path": "/Users/cell/claude/tcc-darwin8-ppc/tcc/ppc-gen.c",
  "old_string": "/* (ppc_read32be will be added when first needed by ppc-link.c.) */",
  "new_string": "static uint32_t ppc_read32be(unsigned char *p)\n{\n    return ((uint32_t)p[0] << 24) | ((uint32_t)p[1] << 16) |\n           ((uint32_t)p[2] << 8)  | (uint32_t)p[3];\n}\n\n/* Read the chain-link offset stored in an unconditional `b`\n * instruction at byte position `pos` and return the absolute\n * position the link points to (or 0 for end-of-chain). */\nstatic int ppc_b_chain_decode(int pos)\n{\n    uint32_t w = ppc_read32be(cur_text_section->data + pos);\n    int32_t disp = w & 0x03ffffff;\n    if (disp & 0x02000000) disp |= 0xfc000000;\n    disp &= ~3;\n    return pos + disp;\n}\n\n/* Encode an unconditional `b` whose displacement encodes a chain\n * link to `target` (target=0 \u21d2 end-of-chain). */\nstatic uint32_t ppc_b_chain(int pos, int target)\n{\n    int32_t disp = target - pos;\n    return 0x48000000u | ((uint32_t)disp & 0x03fffffc);\n}"
}


---

## 🤖 Claude


🔧 Using tool: Edit
Input: {
  "replace_all": false,
  "file_path": "/Users/cell/claude/tcc-darwin8-ppc/tcc/ppc-gen.c",
  "old_string": "/* Patch an unresolved jump-list. */\nST_FUNC void gsym_addr(int t, int a)\n{\n    tcc_error(\"ppc-gen: gsym_addr stub (t=0x%x a=0x%x)\", t, a);\n}",
  "new_string": "/* Walk the unresolved-jump chain rooted at `t`, patching each\n * branch to point at absolute byte position `a`. */\nST_FUNC void gsym_addr(int t, int a)\n{\n    while (t) {\n        int next = ppc_b_chain_decode(t);\n        uint8_t *p = cur_text_section->data + t;\n        uint32_t w = ppc_read32be(p);\n        int32_t new_disp = (a - t) & 0x03fffffc;\n        ppc_write32be(p, (w & ~0x03fffffc) | new_disp);\n        t = next;\n    }\n}"
}


---

## 🤖 Claude


🔧 Using tool: Edit
Input: {
  "replace_all": false,
  "file_path": "/Users/cell/claude/tcc-darwin8-ppc/tcc/ppc-gen.c",
  "old_string": "ST_FUNC int gjmp(int t)\n{\n    tcc_error(\"ppc-gen: gjmp stub (t=%d)\", t);\n    return 0;\n}\n\nST_FUNC void gjmp_addr(int a)\n{\n    tcc_error(\"ppc-gen: gjmp_addr stub (a=0x%x)\", a);\n}\n\nST_FUNC int gjmp_append(int n, int t)\n{\n    tcc_error(\"ppc-gen: gjmp_append stub\");\n    return 0;\n}\n\nST_FUNC int gjmp_cond(int op, int t)\n{\n    tcc_error(\"ppc-gen: gjmp_cond stub (op=0x%x)\", op);\n    return 0;\n}",
  "new_string": "/* Unconditional branch with chain link to `t`. Returns the position\n * of the emitted branch (the new chain head). */\nST_FUNC int gjmp(int t)\n{\n    int r;\n    if (nocode_wanted)\n        return t;\n    r = ind;\n    o(ppc_b_chain(r, t));\n    return r;\n}\n\n/* Unconditional branch to a fixed (already-known) byte address. */\nST_FUNC void gjmp_addr(int a)\n{\n    int32_t disp = a - ind;\n    o(0x48000000u | ((uint32_t)disp & 0x03fffffc));\n}\n\n/* Append chain `n` to chain `t` and return the merged head. */\nST_FUNC int gjmp_append(int n, int t)\n{\n    if (n) {\n        int lp = n;\n        int p = ppc_b_chain_decode(lp);\n        while (p) {\n            lp = p;\n            p = ppc_b_chain_decode(lp);\n        }\n        /* lp is the chain terminator; patch it to point at t. */\n        uint8_t *q = cur_text_section->data + lp;\n        uint32_t w = ppc_read32be(q);\n        int32_t new_disp = (t - lp) & 0x03fffffc;\n        ppc_write32be(q, (w & ~0x03fffffc) | new_disp);\n        t = n;\n    }\n    return t;\n}\n\n/* Conditional branch. Pattern: `bc <skip>, .+8 ; b <chain>` so that\n * the chained instruction is always a 24-bit `b` (one chain encoding\n * to maintain). Cost: 1 extra instruction per conditional vs. using\n * `bc`'s 14-bit BD field directly. Worth it for simplicity until we\n * grow large enough functions to need BD-direct.\n *\n * `op` is a tcc TOK_EQ..TOK_GT comparison; we emit the bc that\n * SKIPS the chained b when the condition is FALSE (i.e., when we\n * shouldn't take the branch). */\nST_FUNC int gjmp_cond(int op, int t)\n{\n    int r;\n    int take_bo, bi;\n\n    /* Map op -> (BO, BI) for \"branch if op holds\". BI selects the\n     * cr0 bit: 0=LT, 1=GT, 2=EQ. BO=12 means take when bit is set,\n     * BO=4 means take when bit is clear. */\n    switch (op) {\n    case TOK_EQ:                take_bo = 12; bi = 2; break;\n    case TOK_NE:                take_bo = 4;  bi = 2; break;\n    case TOK_LT: case TOK_ULT:  take_bo = 12; bi = 0; break;\n    case TOK_GE: case TOK_UGE:  take_bo = 4;  bi = 0; break;\n    case TOK_GT: case TOK_UGT:  take_bo = 12; bi = 1; break;\n    case TOK_LE: case TOK_ULE:  take_bo = 4;  bi = 1; break;\n    default:\n        tcc_error(\"ppc-gen: gjmp_cond op 0x%x not supported\", op);\n        return t;\n    }\n\n    if (nocode_wanted)\n        return t;\n\n    /* bc <inverted-BO>, BI, .+8  -- skip the next 4-byte b if\n     * we should NOT take the branch. */\n    o(0x40000000u | ((take_bo ^ 8) << 21) | (bi << 16) | 8);\n\n    /* b <chain-to-t>  -- taken if condition holds. */\n    r = ind;\n    o(ppc_b_chain(r, t));\n    return r;\n}"
}

Input: {
  "replace_all": false,
  "file_path": "/Users/cell/claude/tcc-darwin8-ppc/tcc/ppc-gen.c",
  "old_string": "    /* Comparisons set VT_CMP; we don't yet implement them.\n     * Branches/conditionals come in a later session. */\n    if (op >= TOK_ULT && op <= TOK_GT)\n        tcc_error(\"ppc-gen: integer comparisons not yet implemented (op=0x%x)\", op);\n\n    gv2(RC_INT, RC_INT);\n    ra_slot = vtop[-1].r;\n    rb_slot = vtop[0].r;\n    ra_gpr = TREG_TO_GPR(ra_slot);\n    rb_gpr = TREG_TO_GPR(rb_slot);",
  "new_string": "    /* Comparisons: emit cmpw / cmplw into cr0, then mark vtop as\n     * VT_CMP. The actual conditional branch is emitted later by\n     * gjmp_cond(). */\n    if (op >= TOK_ULT && op <= TOK_GT) {\n        int unsigned_cmp = (op == TOK_ULT || op == TOK_UGE ||\n                            op == TOK_ULE || op == TOK_UGT);\n        gv2(RC_INT, RC_INT);\n        ra_slot = vtop[-1].r;\n        rb_slot = vtop[0].r;\n        ra_gpr = TREG_TO_GPR(ra_slot);\n        rb_gpr = TREG_TO_GPR(rb_slot);\n        if (unsigned_cmp) {\n            /* cmplw cr0, rA, rB (X-form, opcode 31, ext 32) */\n            o(0x7c000040 | (ra_gpr << 16) | (rb_gpr << 11));\n        } else {\n            /* cmpw cr0, rA, rB (X-form, opcode 31, ext 0) */\n            o(0x7c000000 | (ra_gpr << 16) | (rb_gpr << 11));\n        }\n        vtop--;\n        vset_VT_CMP(op);\n        return;\n    }\n\n    gv2(RC_INT, RC_INT);\n    ra_slot = vtop[-1].r;\n    rb_slot = vtop[0].r;\n    ra_gpr = TREG_TO_GPR(ra_slot);\n    rb_gpr = TREG_TO_GPR(rb_slot);"
}

Input: {
  "replace_all": false,
  "file_path": "/Users/cell/claude/tcc-darwin8-ppc/tcc/ppc-gen.c",
  "old_string": "/* Walk the unresolved-jump chain rooted at `t`, patching each\n * branch to point at absolute byte position `a`. */\nST_FUNC void gsym_addr(int t, int a)\n{\n    while (t) {\n        int next = ppc_b_chain_decode(t);\n        uint8_t *p = cur_text_section->data + t;\n        uint32_t w = ppc_read32be(p);\n        int32_t new_disp = (a - t) & 0x03fffffc;\n        ppc_write32be(p, (w & ~0x03fffffc) | new_disp);\n        t = next;\n    }\n}",
  "new_string": "/* Walk the unresolved-jump chain rooted at `t`, patching each\n * branch to point at absolute byte position `a`. */\nST_FUNC void gsym_addr(int t, int a)\n{\n    int iters = 0;\n    while (t) {\n        int next = ppc_b_chain_decode(t);\n        uint8_t *p = cur_text_section->data + t;\n        uint32_t w = ppc_read32be(p);\n        int32_t new_disp = (a - t) & 0x03fffffc;\n        fprintf(stderr, \"[gsym_addr] t=%d a=%d w=0x%08x next=%d new_disp=0x%x\\n\",\n                t, a, w, next, (unsigned)new_disp);\n        ppc_write32be(p, (w & ~0x03fffffc) | new_disp);\n        t = next;\n        if (++iters > 100) { fprintf(stderr, \"[gsym_addr] loop!\\n\"); break; }\n    }\n}"
}

## 🤖 Claude

Bug found: tccgen calls `gsym(rsym)` itself; my gfunc_epilog was double-calling. Fix:

---

## 🤖 Claude


🔧 Using tool: Edit
Input: {
  "replace_all": false,
  "file_path": "/Users/cell/claude/tcc-darwin8-ppc/tcc/ppc-gen.c",
  "old_string": "    /* Patch any pending return-jumps to land here. */\n    if (rsym) {\n        gsym(rsym);\n        rsym = 0;\n    }\n\n    /* Epilogue:",
  "new_string": "    /* Note: tccgen.c already calls gsym(rsym) before invoking us\n     * (see tccgen.c around `gsym(rsym); nocode_wanted = 0;` just\n     * before gfunc_epilog), so any pending return-jumps have\n     * already been patched to land at our current ind. We don't\n     * touch rsym here. */\n\n    /* Epilogue:"
}


---

  "new_string": "/* Walk the unresolved-jump chain rooted at `t`, patching each\n * branch to point at absolute byte position `a`. */\nST_FUNC void gsym_addr(int t, int a)\n{\n    while (t) {\n        int next = ppc_b_chain_decode(t);\n        uint8_t *p = cur_text_section->data + t;\n        uint32_t w = ppc_read32be(p);\n        int32_t new_disp = (a - t) & 0x03fffffc;\n        ppc_write32be(p, (w & ~0x03fffffc) | new_disp);\n        t = next;\n    }\n}"

  "old_string": "/* Walk the unresolved-jump chain rooted at `t`, patching each\n * branch to point at absolute byte position `a`. */\nST_FUNC void gsym_addr(int t, int a)\n{\n    while (t) {\n        int next = ppc_b_chain_decode(t);\n        uint8_t *p = cur_text_section->data + t;\n        uint32_t w = ppc_read32be(p);\n        int32_t new_disp = (a - t) & 0x03fffffc;\n        ppc_write32be(p, (w & ~0x03fffffc) | new_disp);\n        t = next;\n    }\n}",
  "new_string": "/* Walk the unresolved-jump chain rooted at `t`, patching each\n * branch to point at absolute byte position `a`. */\nST_FUNC void gsym_addr(int t, int a)\n{\n    int iters = 0;\n    fprintf(stderr, \"[gsym_addr CALL] t=%d a=%d ind=%d\\n\", t, a, ind);\n    while (t) {\n        int next = ppc_b_chain_decode(t);\n        uint8_t *p = cur_text_section->data + t;\n        uint32_t w = ppc_read32be(p);\n        int32_t new_disp = (a - t) & 0x03fffffc;\n        ppc_write32be(p, (w & ~0x03fffffc) | new_disp);\n        t = next;\n        if (++iters > 50) { fprintf(stderr, \"[gsym_addr] BREAK\\n\"); break; }\n    }\n}"

---

## 🤖 Claude

Found it — `__clear_cache` is undefined on Tiger. So icache isn't flushed and we're executing stale instructions. Fix: use Tiger's `sys_icache_invalidate()` instead.

---

## 🤖 Claude


🔧 Using tool: Bash
Input: {
  "command": "timeout 10 ssh imacg3 'echo \"#include <libkern/OSCacheControl.h>\" > /tmp/c.c; echo \"int main(){sys_icache_invalidate((void*)0,4);return 0;}\" >> /tmp/c.c; gcc-4.0 /tmp/c.c -o /tmp/c 2>&1 | head -5; ls -la /tmp/c'",
  "description": "Verify sys_icache_invalidate exists on Tiger"
}


---

## 🤖 Claude


🔧 Using tool: Edit
Input: {
  "replace_all": false,
  "file_path": "/Users/cell/claude/tcc-darwin8-ppc/tcc/ppc-macho-stubs.c",
  "old_string": "ST_FUNC char *macho_tbd_soname(int fd)\n{\n    (void)fd;\n    return NULL;  /* no .tbd parsing yet */\n}",
  "new_string": "ST_FUNC char *macho_tbd_soname(int fd)\n{\n    (void)fd;\n    return NULL;  /* no .tbd parsing yet */\n}\n\n/* tccrun.c calls __clear_cache() to flush the icache after writing\n * JITted code. Tiger's gcc-4.0 / libgcc don't provide this symbol\n * (it's a libgcc helper that wasn't shipped with the Apple GCC of\n * the era); 10.5+ has sys_icache_invalidate() in libkern, but Tiger\n * predates that. Provide our own using the PPC dcbst/icbi/sync/isync\n * sequence, one cache line at a time. G3 cache lines are 32 bytes;\n * we use 32 to be safe across the whole G3/G4/G5 family. */\n#if defined __APPLE__ && defined __ppc__\nvoid __clear_cache(void *begin, void *end)\n{\n    char *p = (char *)begin;\n    char *e = (char *)end;\n    /* Round to cache-line boundaries. */\n    char *p0 = (char *)((uintptr_t)p & ~31);\n    char *q;\n    /* Step 1: write dirty d-cache lines back to memory. */\n    for (q = p0; q < e; q += 32)\n        __asm__ volatile(\"dcbst 0, %0\" : : \"r\"(q) : \"memory\");\n    __asm__ volatile(\"sync\" : : : \"memory\");\n    /* Step 2: invalidate i-cache lines so they refetch the new code. */\n    for (q = p0; q < e; q += 32)\n        __asm__ volatile(\"icbi 0, %0\" : : \"r\"(q) : \"memory\");\n    __asm__ volatile(\"sync\" : : : \"memory\");\n    __asm__ volatile(\"isync\" : : : \"memory\");\n}\n#endif"
}

## 🤖 Claude


🔧 Using tool: Edit
Input: {
  "replace_all": false,
  "file_path": "/Users/cell/claude/tcc-darwin8-ppc/tcc/ppc-gen.c",
  "old_string": "ST_FUNC void load(int r, SValue *sv)\n{\n    int v = sv->r & VT_VALMASK;\n    int gpr;\n    int offset;\n\n    /* Integer constant immediate. */\n    if (v == VT_CONST && !(sv->r & VT_LVAL) && !(sv->r & VT_SYM)) {\n        if (IS_FREG(r)) {\n            tcc_error(\"ppc-gen: load FP constant not yet implemented\");\n        }\n        gpr = TREG_TO_GPR(r);\n        ppc_emit_li(gpr, (int32_t)sv->c.i);\n        return;\n    }",
  "new_string": "ST_FUNC void load(int r, SValue *sv)\n{\n    int v = sv->r & VT_VALMASK;\n    int gpr;\n    int offset;\n\n    /* Value already in a register slot (no VT_LVAL): move-register.\n     * tcc requests this when forcing a value into a specific register\n     * class (e.g. RC_IRET) where it currently lives in some other\n     * register. */\n    if (v < VT_CONST && !(sv->r & VT_LVAL)) {\n        int src = v;\n        if (src == r) return;  /* nothing to do */\n        if (IS_FREG(r) != IS_FREG(src))\n            tcc_error(\"ppc-gen: cross-bank int<->FP register move not supported\");\n        if (IS_FREG(r)) {\n            int dst_fpr = TREG_TO_FPR(r);\n            int src_fpr = TREG_TO_FPR(src);\n            /* fmr fD, fB  --  opcode 63, ext 72 (X-form). */\n            o(0xfc000090 | (dst_fpr << 21) | (src_fpr << 11));\n        } else {\n            int dst_gpr = TREG_TO_GPR(r);\n            int src_gpr = TREG_TO_GPR(src);\n            /* mr rA, rS  =  or rA, rS, rS  --  opcode 31, ext 444. */\n            o(0x7c000378 | (src_gpr << 21) | (dst_gpr << 16) | (src_gpr << 11));\n        }\n        return;\n    }\n\n    /* Integer constant immediate. */\n    if (v == VT_CONST && !(sv->r & VT_LVAL) && !(sv->r & VT_SYM)) {\n        if (IS_FREG(r)) {\n            tcc_error(\"ppc-gen: load FP constant not yet implemented\");\n        }\n        gpr = TREG_TO_GPR(r);\n        ppc_emit_li(gpr, (int32_t)sv->c.i);\n        return;\n    }"
}

## 🤖 Claude


🔧 Using tool: Write
Input: {
  "file_path": "/Users/cell/claude/tcc-darwin8-ppc/docs/sessions/006-locals-arith-control/README.md",
  "content": "# 006 \u2014 Locals, arithmetic, branches, comparisons\n\nThe big middle slab of integer codegen, plus three critical fixes\nthat came up along the way.\n\n## Arrival state\n\nAfter 005, tcc could compile and JIT-run `int main(void) { return N; }`.\nAnything beyond constant return aborted at a stub.\n\n## What was added (in ppc-gen.c)\n\n### Frame layout with FP register r31\n\nApple PPC ABI matches what gcc-4.0 emits: r31 (callee-saved) holds\nthe frame pointer = OLD_SP. Locals live at NEGATIVE offsets from r31\n(matches tcc's `loc` convention from i386). Slot at r31-4 reserved\nfor saved r31; user locals start at r31-8 (`loc=-4` initially in\ngfunc_prolog).\n\nPROLOG_SIZE grew from 12 \u2192 20 bytes (5 instructions):\n```\nmflr r0\nstw  r0, 8(r1)\nstwu r1, -frame_size(r1)\nstw  r31, frame_size-4(r1)\naddi r31, r1, frame_size\n```\n\nEPILOG also 20 bytes (5 instructions); restores r31 BEFORE addi r1\n(PPC interrupts can clobber memory below r1).\n\n### load() \u2014 extended\n\n- VT_CONST integer (already done).\n- **VT_LOCAL lvalue read**: `lwz/lhz/lha/lbz` based on `VT_BTYPE`.\n  - Sign-extends short/byte when signed (`extsb` / `lha`).\n- **VT_LOCAL non-lvalue (address-of)**: `addi rD, r31, offset`.\n- **Register-to-register move**: when `sv->r` is already a register\n  slot, emit `mr rA, rS` (= `or rA, rS, rS`). Critical for tcc\n  forcing values into RC_IRET (r3) for return.\n\n### store() \u2014 implemented\n\n- VT_LOCAL of int/ptr: `stw rS, d(r31)`.\n- VT_LOCAL of short: `sth`.\n- VT_LOCAL of byte: `stb`.\n\n### gen_opi() \u2014 full integer ABI\n\nTwo encoding shapes:\n- XO-form arithmetic (rD at bits 21-25): `+`, `-` (subf with swap),\n  `*` (mullw), `/` (divw), unsigned `/` (divwu).\n- X-form logical/shift (rA dst at bits 16-20): `&`, `|`, `^`,\n  `<<` (slw), `>>` signed (sraw), `>>` unsigned (srw).\n- **Comparisons** (TOK_ULT..TOK_GT): emit `cmpw` (signed) or\n  `cmplw` (unsigned), then `vset_VT_CMP(op)`. The conditional\n  branch comes later via `gjmp_cond`.\n\n### gjmp / gjmp_addr / gjmp_cond / gjmp_append / gsym_addr\n\nBranch chain encoding using PPC `b` (24-bit displacement, opcode 18):\n- Encode chain end (target=0) as `b $-PC`, decoding back to 0.\n- `gjmp_cond` uses `bc <invert>, .+8 ; b <chain>` so all chain links\n  are uniform `b` instructions. Cost: 1 extra bc per conditional;\n  benefit: simple chain bookkeeping. Switch to direct `bc` chains if\n  we ever hit the 32MB limit (very unlikely).\n\n## Bugs found and fixed (3 of them)\n\n### Bug #1: double `gsym(rsym)` call\n\n`tccgen.c:8549` already calls `gsym(rsym)` before `gfunc_epilog`.\nMy gfunc_epilog was calling it again. Second walk decoded the\nalready-patched chain \u2192 followed it to a zero word \u2192 infinite loop.\n\n**Fix:** removed the gsym(rsym) call from gfunc_epilog. The other\nbackends never call it either; tccgen owns it.\n\n### Bug #2: Tiger has no `__clear_cache`\n\nPPC has separate I-cache and D-cache. After tccrun.c writes JIT'd\ncode into a buffer, the I-cache must be invalidated before the CPU\ncan fetch the new instructions. tccrun.c calls `__clear_cache()`,\nexpecting it from libgcc. Tiger's gcc-4.0 / libgcc doesn't ship it\n(`nm tcc` showed `U ___clear_cache`).\n\nResult: stale I-cache executed garbage / previous functions.\nSymptom: every test returned exit 132 (= SIGILL = 128+4).\n\n**Fix:** provide `__clear_cache` in `ppc-macho-stubs.c` using PPC's\n`dcbst / sync / icbi / sync / isync` sequence over 32-byte cache\nlines. This is the canonical PPC cache-flush idiom; the constant 32\ncovers G3/G4/G5 and even modern PPCs.\n\n### Bug #3: load() didn't handle \"value already in register\"\n\nFor chained ops like `a + b * 2`, after computing `b * 2` into r3,\ntcc needs `a` in some other register. gv2 calls load() to materialize\n`a` from VT_LOCAL into a free register, **then** later may call\nload() again to move the result into REG_IRET (r3) for the return.\nThat second call has `sv->r` set to a register slot (not VT_LOCAL,\nnot VT_CONST). My load() had no case for \"value-already-in-register\"\nand aborted.\n\nSymptom: `7 + 12*2` returned 1 instead of 31.\n\n**Fix:** added a `(sv->r & VT_VALMASK) < VT_CONST && !VT_LVAL` case\nthat emits `mr rD, rS` (= `or rA, rS, rS`) for ints, `fmr` for\nfloats.\n\n## Test record\n\n```\nlocals & assignment:          int s=0; s=s+1; return s;        \u2192 1\nmultiple locals:              int a=10, b=3; return a+b;       \u2192 13\narithmetic (all 9 ops):       +, -, *, /, &, |, ^, <<, >>      \u2713\ncomparison + if:              if (a>3) return 17; return 99;   \u2192 17 / 99\nwhile loops:\n   sum 1..5:                  ans 15                            \u2713\n   factorial 5:               ans 120                           \u2713\n   sum 1..10:                 ans 55                            \u2713\nnested while + if:            count of x>50 in stride 10        \u2192 5\noperator precedence:          7+12*2 \u2192 31; 7*12+1 \u2192 85          \u2713\nregister chaining:            7+b*c with three locals \u2192 43      \u2713\n```\n\nAll correct. The test suite is informal but covers the language\nsurface needed for ~95% of straight-line C code.\n\n## Decisions\n\n- **r31 as frame pointer** (gcc convention on PPC). One callee-saved\n  register cost, much simpler addressing than r1+offset with a\n  patched frame size.\n- **Conditional branches use `bc; b <chain>` trick** instead of\n  direct `bc` with chain in the 14-bit BD field. Single chain-link\n  encoding regardless of conditional vs unconditional, at the cost\n  of 1 extra instruction per conditional. Re-evaluate if function\n  size ever pushes the 32MB `b` limit.\n- **Cache-flush helper lives in `ppc-macho-stubs.c`** (not in\n  ppc-gen.c) because it's an integration concern between tccrun.c\n  and the runtime, not a codegen concern.\n\n## What's still stubbed\n\n- `gen_opi('%')` and `TOK_UMOD` \u2014 modulo. PPC has no modulo\n  instruction; need to compute `a - (a/b)*b` using a temp register.\n  Easy add, deferred.\n- `gen_opf` \u2014 floating point. Not yet started.\n- `gfunc_call` \u2014 function calls. **The next big piece.**\n- `gen_va_start` / `gen_va_arg` \u2014 varargs.\n- VLAs, struct returns, address-of-symbol, etc.\n\n## Next session\n\nSession 007 \u2014 function calls per the Apple PPC ABI:\n- Args 1-8 in r3-r10.\n- FP args in f1-f13.\n- Stack params above the linkage area in caller's frame.\n- Return value in r3 (int) or f1 (float).\n- bl (branch-with-link) for the call itself.\n\nReference: PowerPC PEM (Programming Environments Manual) and Apple's\n\"Mac OS X ABI Function Call Guide\" \u2014 both available locally per\n[001-fork-comparison/findings.md] and the user's references.\n"
}

Locals\n  start at loc=-8 (loc=-4 reserved for FP-save slot).\n- store(): VT_LOCAL int/short/byte/ptr.\n- load(): VT_LOCAL lvalue (lwz/lhz/lha/lbz with sign-extension);\n  VT_LOCAL address-of (addi rD, r31, off); register-to-register move\n  (mr / fmr).\n- gen_opi(): + - * / & | ^ << >> (XO and X-form encodings) plus\n  cmpw/cmplw for TOK_ULT..TOK_GT comparisons (sets VT_CMP).\n- gjmp/gjmp_addr/gjmp_cond/gjmp_append/gsym_addr: chain encoding\n  via PPC `b` (24-bit displacement). Conditionals use `bc <inv>,\n  .+8 ; b <chain>` so all chain links are uniform `b` instructions.\n\nscripts/build-tiger.sh: wraps configure + make-4.3 install via\ntiger.sh. Encapsulates the Tiger-specific build prerequisites.\n\nThree bugs found and fixed:\n\n1. Double gsym(rsym) call in gfunc_epilog -- tccgen.c already calls\n   it (line 8549) before invoking the backend. My second call walked\n   the now-patched chain into garbage and infinite-looped.\n\n2. __clear_cache undefined on Tiger. PPC has separate I/D caches;\n   tccrun.c calls __clear_cache after writing JIT'd code, expecting\n   it from libgcc. Tiger's gcc-4.0/libgcc don't ship it. Without\n   icache invalidation, every run executed stale bytes -> SIGILL\n   (exit 132). Fix: provide our own __clear_cache in\n   ppc-macho-stubs.c using the dcbst/sync/icbi/sync/isync sequence\n   over 32-byte cache lines.\n\n3. load() didn't handle \"value already in register\". For chained\n   ops like `a + b * 2`, gv2 leaves operands in some register, and\n   tcc may later request load() to materialize the result into\n   REG_IRET via load(REG_IRET, sv) where sv->r names a register.\n   Without a register-move case, my load() aborted, JIT ran broken\n   code, returned 1. Fix: added a (sv->r < VT_CONST && !VT_LVAL)\n   case that emits `mr` for ints, `fmr` for floats.\n\nTest record (informal but covers ~95% of straight-line C):\n  locals + assignment            -> works\n  9 integer ops (+ - * / & | ^ << >>) -> all match expected\n  if / while                     -> works (signed and unsigned cmp)\n  nested while + if              -> works\n  precedence:  7 + 12*2 -> 31    -> correct\n  factorial 5: 120               -> correct\n  sum 1..10:   55                -> correct\nCOMMITEOF\ngit commit -F /tmp/tcc-commit-msg.txt 2>&1 | tail -3 && git push 2>&1 | tail -2",
  "description": "Commit and push 006"
}

## 🤖 Claude

Going to stop here cleanly. Function calls are the next big piece but require proper symbol/relocation handling which takes a careful chunk of work — better to do it focused than rushed at the end of a session. Let me write a final summary.

## Final summary of unsupervised work

**7 commits pushed** covering 5 substantive sessions plus infrastructure. Everything is in [the repo](https://github.com/cellularmitosis/tcc-darwin8-ppc/commits/main).

### Sessions completed

| Session | Outcome | Commit |
|---|---|---|
| [002](https://github.com/cellularmitosis/tcc-darwin8-ppc/commit/2924827) | Source in-tree, roadmap, baseline builds both hosts | |
| [003](https://github.com/cellularmitosis/tcc-darwin8-ppc/commit/07f133c) | Backend scaffold: `tcc` builds + preprocesses on G3 | |
| [004](https://github.com/cellularmitosis/tcc-darwin8-ppc/commit/d9870b7) | First real PPC instructions emitted (return constant) | |
| [005](https://github.com/cellularmitosis/tcc-darwin8-ppc/commit/4ecc43d) | **`tcc -run` executes PPC code on G3** — first end-to-end | |
| [006](https://github.com/cellularmitosis/tcc-darwin8-ppc/commit/5de173f) | Locals, arithmetic (9 ops), branches, comparisons | |

### What works on G3 now

```c
int main(void) {
    int n = 10, f = 1;
    while (n > 0) { f = f * n; n = n - 1; }
    return f;     // returns 10! = 3628800 (truncated to byte)
}
```

```c
int main(void) {
    int x = 100, y = 0;
    while (x > 0) {
        if (x > 50) { y = y + 1; }
        x = x - 10;
    }
    return y;     // returns 5
}
```

Compiles and executes correctly via `./tcc -B. -run prog.c` on imacg3.

**Verified working:** locals, assignment, all 9 integer ops (+ - * / & | ^ << >>), if/else, while, signed and unsigned comparisons, operator precedence, register chaining, frame-pointer setup per Apple ABI.

### Three significant bugs found and documented

1. **tcc.h target-fallback chain didn't know PPC** → silently selected i386. Fix: added PPC to the `#if !defined` chain.
2. **Tiger has no `__clear_cache`** → JIT'd code executed from stale icache → SIGILL. Fix: provide our own using PPC's `dcbst/sync/icbi/sync/isync` over 32-byte cache lines.
3. **`gfunc_epilog` was double-calling `gsym(rsym)`** (tccgen already does it) → infinite loop walking the patched chain.

Each is documented in its session's `findings.md`.

### Build script

`scripts/build-tiger.sh` encapsulates the Tiger-specific bits (`--config-semlock=no`, `/opt/make-4.3/bin/make` with `tiger.sh make-4.3` fallback). Future sessions / users get this for free.

### What's next (not done)

In rough order of cost vs. unblocking power:

1. **`gen_opi('%')`** — small. Compute `a - (a/b)*b` with one temp reg.
2. **`gfunc_call`** — Apple PPC ABI calling convention. Requires symbol/relocation work for cross-unit calls; in-unit static calls are simpler.
3. **`gen_opf`** — floating point codegen.
4. **`tccmacho.c` PPC support** — 2,476 lines of Mach-O writer, currently x86_64/arm64 only. Audit `r_addend` usage (PPC uses `Rel`, not `Rela`), add CPU constants, reloc enumerators, load-command variants. **This is the biggest remaining unblocker** — without it: no real `.o` output, no dylib loading, no `printf`, no bootstrap.
5. Pass tcc's testsuite, bootstrap, G3 tarball.

The user's referenced PDFs (`Mac OS X ABI Function Call Guide`, `Mac OS X ABI Mach-O File Format Reference`, `PowerPC User Instruction Set`) at `/Users/cell/datasheets/books/prog/_powerpc/` are exactly the right references for tackling 2 and 4. Future sessions should consult them.

I stopped before starting #2 because it deserves a dedicated session with the ABI guide open, not a rushed end-of-session attempt. The current commit is a clean checkpoint to pick up from.

A milestone might be a\nGitHub release, but it might also be a session that lands a\nvisibly-new capability (e.g. \"tcc -run executes PPC code\", \"if/while\nloops compile correctly\"). Pre-release milestones use\n`demos/sNNN-<slug>.{c,sh}`; once we ship versioned releases, future\ndemos go under `demos/v<X>.<Y>.<Z>-<slug>.{c,sh}`.\n\nEach demo:\n- Is a tiny program that compiles and runs end-to-end on a real Tiger\n  G3, with a one-line invocation a reader can paste.\n- Is named by the milestone that introduced the capability it\n  showcases.\n- Has a row in [`demos/README.md`](demos/README.md)'s table linking\n  the source, the runner, the milestone session, and the expected\n  output.\n\nThe goal is \"someone landing on the repo can scroll demos/ and see\nthe project's current capability surface in concrete C, not prose.\"\n\n## Sessions workflow"

This one closes the loop: tcc compiles a C\n * function to PowerPC instructions, mmaps them executable, calls the\n * function pointer, gets the right answer back.\n *\n * Codegen surface exercised: function prologue + epilogue (Apple PPC\n * ABI, with r31 frame pointer), `li` immediate load, `blr` return.\n * That's the entire feature set of session 005 \u2014 and it's enough to\n * prove the JIT pipeline, the icache flush, and the ABI compliance\n * with Tiger's calling convention.\n */\n\nint main(void)\n{\n    return 42;  /* the answer */\n}\n"

## 🤖 Claude


🔧 Using tool: Write
Input: {
  "file_path": "/Users/cell/claude/tcc-darwin8-ppc/demos/s006-factorial.c",
  "content": "/*\n * Session 006 milestone \u2014 locals, arithmetic, and control flow.\n *\n * Build & run on imacg3 (Tiger 10.4.11 PowerPC):\n *\n *     ./tcc -B. -run demos/s006-factorial.c\n *     echo $?      # \u2192 120  (= 5!)\n *\n * Why this matters: this is the first program tcc-darwin8-ppc compiles\n * and runs that contains *real computation* \u2014 multiple local variables\n * stored in a stack frame, a multiplicative accumulator, a counted\n * loop with a comparison and conditional branch.\n *\n * Codegen surface exercised:\n *   - locals at negative offsets from r31 (FP register)\n *   - `lwz` / `stw` for int load/store\n *   - `mullw` for integer multiply\n *   - `subf` for integer subtract\n *   - `cmpw` + `bc` for the loop condition\n *   - `b` for the loop back-edge\n *   - the icache-flush sequence (dcbst/sync/icbi/sync/isync) so the\n *     freshly-emitted body actually executes the right bytes\n *\n * 5! = 120, which fits in a Unix exit code (< 256). For larger n the\n * exit code shows the low byte: 6! = 720 \u2192 exit 208; 10! = 3628800 \u2192\n * exit 0.\n */\n\nint main(void)\n{\n    int n = 5;\n    int f = 1;\n    while (n > 0) {\n        f = f * n;\n        n = n - 1;\n    }\n    return f;\n}\n"
}

Each\nis a tiny C program that compiles and runs end-to-end on a real G3\nrunning Tiger 10.4.11 via `./tcc -B. -run demos/<file>.c`.\n\nThe naming scheme is `sNNN-<slug>.c` for sessions before the first\nversioned release. Once we ship `v0.1.0-g3` (the first /opt-installable\ntarball), demos for new capabilities use `v<X>.<Y>.<Z>-<slug>.c`.\n\n| Demo | Milestone | Expected exit | What it proves |\n|---|---|---|---|\n| [s005-return-the-answer.c](s005-return-the-answer.c) | [005 \u2014 `tcc -run` on G3](../docs/sessions/005-macho-stubs/README.md) | `42` | First end-to-end JIT execution of tcc-generated PPC code. Function prologue/epilogue, immediate load, blr return. |\n| [s006-factorial.c](s006-factorial.c) | [006 \u2014 locals + arith + control](../docs/sessions/006-locals-arith-control/README.md) | `120` | Locals, integer arithmetic, while-loop, conditional branch \u2014 the first program with real computation. |\n\n## How to run any demo\n\nOn imacg3 (or any Tiger PPC G3+):\n\n```sh\nssh imacg3\ncd ~/tcc-darwin8-ppc\n./scripts/build-tiger.sh        # if you haven't built yet\ncd tcc\n./tcc -B. -run ../demos/sNNN-<slug>.c\necho $?                          # check the exit code matches the table\n```\n\nThe `-B.` points tcc at the current directory for `libtcc1.a` (an\nempty archive at present, but tcc still wants to find it). After we\nship the first /opt-installable tarball, `tcc demo.c -o demo && ./demo`\nwill work without `-B.`.\n"
