Input: {
  "file_path": "/tmp/exe_new.c",
  "content": "/* Helper: locate _main's offset within the .text section. */\nstatic int exe_find_main_offset(TCCState *s1, Section *text)\n{\n    int nsyms = s1->symtab->data_offset / sizeof(ElfW(Sym));\n    int i;\n    for (i = 1; i < nsyms; i++) {\n        ElfW(Sym) *sym = &((ElfW(Sym) *)s1->symtab->data)[i];\n        const char *name = (char *)s1->symtab->link->data + sym->st_name;\n        if (sym->st_shndx == text->sh_num && name\n            && (!strcmp(name, \"_main\") || !strcmp(name, \"main\")))\n            return (int)sym->st_value;\n    }\n    return -1;\n}\n\n/* Helper: emit the crt-shim into `text` after the user code. The shim:\n *   bl   _main            (REL24, displacement = main_off - shim_off)\n *   li   r0, 1            (SYS_exit)\n *   sc\n *   trap                  (defensive)\n * Returns the shim's offset within the section. */\nstatic int exe_emit_crt_shim(TCCState *s1, unsigned char *text_data,\n                             int *text_size, int main_off)\n{\n    int shim_off = *text_size;\n    int disp;\n    uint32_t bl_word;\n    uint32_t i;\n\n    disp = main_off - shim_off;\n    if (disp < -(1 << 25) || disp >= (1 << 25)) {\n        tcc_error_noabort(\"ppc-macho: crt shim too far from main (disp=0x%x)\",\n                          (unsigned)disp);\n        return -1;\n    }\n    bl_word = 0x48000001u | ((uint32_t)disp & 0x03fffffc);\n\n    i = shim_off;\n    text_data[i+0] = (bl_word >> 24) & 0xff;\n    text_data[i+1] = (bl_word >> 16) & 0xff;\n    text_data[i+2] = (bl_word >>  8) & 0xff;\n    text_data[i+3] =  bl_word        & 0xff;\n    text_data[i+4] = 0x38; text_data[i+5] = 0x00;\n    text_data[i+6] = 0x00; text_data[i+7] = 0x01;\n    text_data[i+8] = 0x44; text_data[i+9] = 0x00;\n    text_data[i+10]= 0x00; text_data[i+11]= 0x02;\n    text_data[i+12]= 0x7f; text_data[i+13]= 0xe0;\n    text_data[i+14]= 0x00; text_data[i+15]= 0x08;\n\n    *text_size = shim_off + 16;\n    return shim_off;\n}\n\n/* Per-section layout info used during EXE writing. */\nstruct exe_sect {\n    Section  *elf;          /* tcc Section, or NULL for synthesized */\n    uint32_t  vmaddr;       /* assigned VM address */\n    uint32_t  size;         /* size in bytes */\n    uint32_t  file_off;     /* file offset (or 0 for zerofill) */\n    int       is_zerofill;\n};\n\n/* Look up the VM address of a symbol given the laid-out sections.\n * For external (undef) symbols that have a stub assigned, returns\n * the stub address. Returns 0 and sets *err on error. */\nstatic uint32_t exe_sym_addr(TCCState *s1, int symidx,\n                             struct exe_sect *sects, int nsec,\n                             struct extern_stub *stubs, int nstubs,\n                             int *stub_for_elfsym,\n                             const char **err_name)\n{\n    ElfW(Sym) *sym = &((ElfW(Sym) *)s1->symtab->data)[symidx];\n    int j;\n    if (sym->st_shndx == SHN_UNDEF) {\n        if (stub_for_elfsym && stub_for_elfsym[symidx] >= 0)\n            return stubs[stub_for_elfsym[symidx]].addr;\n        *err_name = (char *)s1->symtab->link->data + sym->st_name;\n        return 0;\n    }\n    for (j = 0; j < nsec; j++) {\n        if (sects[j].elf && sects[j].elf->sh_num == sym->st_shndx)\n            return sects[j].vmaddr + sym->st_value;\n    }\n    *err_name = (char *)s1->symtab->link->data + sym->st_name;\n    return 0;\n}\n\n/* Resolve relocations within `s` against the laid-out sections and\n * stubs. Modifies the section data buffer in place. Returns 0 on\n * success, -1 on error.\n *\n * `sect_data` is a writable copy of the section's bytes (modified\n * in place). `sect_vmaddr` is where this section will be loaded.\n */\nstatic int exe_resolve_section_relocs(TCCState *s1, Section *s,\n                                      uint32_t sect_vmaddr,\n                                      unsigned char *sect_data,\n                                      struct exe_sect *all_sects,\n                                      int nsec,\n                                      struct extern_stub *stubs,\n                                      int nstubs,\n                                      int *stub_for_elfsym)\n{\n    Section *rel = s->reloc;\n    int nrel, i;\n    ElfW_Rel *rels;\n    if (!rel || !rel->data_offset)\n        return 0;\n    nrel = rel->data_offset / sizeof(ElfW_Rel);\n    rels = (ElfW_Rel *)rel->data;\n    for (i = 0; i < nrel; i++) {\n        int type = ELFW(R_TYPE)(rels[i].r_info);\n        int symidx = ELFW(R_SYM)(rels[i].r_info);\n        uint32_t reloc_off = rels[i].r_offset;\n        uint32_t target_addr;\n        const char *err_name = NULL;\n        uint32_t inst;\n\n        target_addr = exe_sym_addr(s1, symidx, all_sects, nsec,\n                                    stubs, nstubs, stub_for_elfsym,\n                                    &err_name);\n        if (err_name) {\n            tcc_error_noabort(\"ppc-macho: undefined symbol '%s' \"\n                              \"(no stub allocated; only function \"\n                              \"calls via REL24 get stubs in -o exe)\",\n                              err_name);\n            return -1;\n        }\n\n        switch (type) {\n        case R_PPC_REL24: {\n            int32_t bdisp = (int32_t)(target_addr - (sect_vmaddr + reloc_off));\n            if (bdisp < -(1 << 25) || bdisp >= (1 << 25)) {\n                tcc_error_noabort(\"ppc-macho: REL24 out of range\");\n                return -1;\n            }\n            inst = ((uint32_t)sect_data[reloc_off] << 24)\n                 | ((uint32_t)sect_data[reloc_off+1] << 16)\n                 | ((uint32_t)sect_data[reloc_off+2] <<  8)\n                 |  (uint32_t)sect_data[reloc_off+3];\n            inst = (inst & ~0x03fffffc) | ((uint32_t)bdisp & 0x03fffffc);\n            sect_data[reloc_off+0] = (inst >> 24) & 0xff;\n            sect_data[reloc_off+1] = (inst >> 16) & 0xff;\n            sect_data[reloc_off+2] = (inst >>  8) & 0xff;\n            sect_data[reloc_off+3] =  inst        & 0xff;\n            break;\n        }\n        case R_PPC_ADDR16_HA:\n        case R_PPC_ADDR16_LO:\n        case R_PPC_ADDR32: {\n            uint16_t imm;\n            inst = ((uint32_t)sect_data[reloc_off] << 24)\n                 | ((uint32_t)sect_data[reloc_off+1] << 16)\n                 | ((uint32_t)sect_data[reloc_off+2] <<  8)\n                 |  (uint32_t)sect_data[reloc_off+3];\n            if (type == R_PPC_ADDR32) {\n                inst = target_addr;\n            } else if (type == R_PPC_ADDR16_HA) {\n                imm = ((target_addr + 0x8000) >> 16) & 0xffff;\n                inst = (inst & ~0xffff) | imm;\n            } else {\n                imm = target_addr & 0xffff;\n                inst = (inst & ~0xffff) | imm;\n            }\n            sect_data[reloc_off+0] = (inst >> 24) & 0xff;\n            sect_data[reloc_off+1] = (inst >> 16) & 0xff;\n            sect_data[reloc_off+2] = (inst >>  8) & 0xff;\n            sect_data[reloc_off+3] =  inst        & 0xff;\n            break;\n        }\n        case R_PPC_NONE:\n            break;\n        default:\n            tcc_error_noabort(\"ppc-macho: reloc type %d in -o exe \"\n                              \"not yet handled (sym '%s')\", type,\n                              (char *)s1->symtab->link->data\n                              + ((ElfW(Sym) *)s1->symtab->data)[symidx].st_name);\n            return -1;\n        }\n    }\n    return 0;\n}\n\n/* Write a 16-byte section name padded with NULs. */\nstatic void put_sectname(obuf *out, const char *name)\n{\n    char nm[16];\n    memset(nm, 0, sizeof(nm));\n    strncpy(nm, name, sizeof(nm));\n    obuf_put(out, nm, 16);\n}\n\n/* Write a Mach-O nlist entry (12 bytes). */\nstatic void put_nlist(obuf *nlist, uint32_t strx, uint8_t n_type,\n                      uint8_t n_sect, uint16_t n_desc, uint32_t n_value)\n{\n    put32be(nlist, strx);\n    put8(nlist, n_type);\n    put8(nlist, n_sect);\n    put8(nlist, (n_desc >> 8) & 0xff);\n    put8(nlist, n_desc & 0xff);\n    put32be(nlist, n_value);\n}\n\n/* The big one. */\nstatic int macho_output_exe(TCCState *s1, const char *filename)\n{\n    obuf out;\n    obuf nlist, strtab, indirect;\n    Section *text = NULL, *rodata = NULL;\n    int main_off, shim_off;\n    int i, ret = -1;\n    FILE *fp = NULL;\n    int fd;\n\n    /* External-stub bookkeeping (matches the OBJ writer). */\n    struct extern_stub *stubs = NULL;\n    int *stub_for_elfsym = NULL;\n    int nstubs = 0;\n    unsigned char *stub_data = NULL;\n    unsigned char *nl_ptr_data = NULL;\n\n    /* Section index ranges (1-based: __text=1). */\n    enum { SECT_TEXT = 1 };\n    int sect_const = 0, sect_stub = 0, sect_nlptr = 0;\n    int n_text_sects, n_data_sects, total_msects;\n\n    struct exe_sect sects[8];   /* generous; we never use more than 4 */\n    int nsec = 0;\n    int sect_idx_text = -1, sect_idx_rodata = -1;\n    int sect_idx_stub = -1, sect_idx_nlptr = -1;\n\n    /* Layout. */\n    uint32_t text_seg_vmaddr = EXE_TEXT_VMADDR_BASE;\n    uint32_t text_sect_vmaddr;\n    uint32_t hdr_and_lc_size, ncmds, total_cmds_size;\n    uint32_t text_data_size;\n    uint32_t cur_va, cur_off;\n    uint32_t text_seg_filesize, text_seg_vmsize;\n    uint32_t data_seg_vmaddr = 0, data_seg_file_off = 0;\n    uint32_t data_seg_filesize = 0;\n    uint32_t linkedit_vmaddr, linkedit_file_off, linkedit_filesize;\n    uint32_t entry_addr;\n    uint32_t indirect_file_off = 0, sym_file_off, str_file_off;\n\n    /* LC sizes. */\n    uint32_t pagezero_cmd_size = 56;\n    uint32_t text_seg_cmd_size, data_seg_cmd_size = 0;\n    uint32_t linkedit_cmd_size = 56;\n    uint32_t symtab_cmd_size = 24;\n    uint32_t dysymtab_cmd_size = 80;\n    uint32_t unixthread_cmd_size = 8 + 8 + 40 * 4;\n    /* dyld and dylib paths (always nul-terminated, then padded to 4). */\n    static const char dylib_path[] = \"/usr/lib/libSystem.B.dylib\";\n    static const char dyld_path[]  = \"/usr/lib/dyld\";\n    uint32_t dylib_path_aligned = (sizeof(dylib_path) + 3u) & ~3u;\n    uint32_t dyld_path_aligned  = (sizeof(dyld_path)  + 3u) & ~3u;\n    uint32_t dylib_cmd_size = 24 + dylib_path_aligned;\n    uint32_t dyld_cmd_size  = 12 + dyld_path_aligned;\n\n    /* Symtab counts. */\n    int n_localsym = 0, n_extdefsym = 0, n_undefsym = 0;\n    /* For each stub, the index of its symbol within nlist. */\n    int *stub_sym_idx = NULL;\n\n    unsigned char *text_data = NULL, *rodata_data = NULL;\n    int new_text_size;\n\n    memset(&out,      0, sizeof(out));\n    memset(&nlist,    0, sizeof(nlist));\n    memset(&strtab,   0, sizeof(strtab));\n    memset(&indirect, 0, sizeof(indirect));\n    memset(sects,     0, sizeof(sects));\n\n    /* ---- Find sections. ---- */\n    for (i = 1; i < s1->nb_sections; i++) {\n        Section *s = s1->sections[i];\n        if (!s) continue;\n        if (s->sh_type == SHT_PROGBITS && (s->sh_flags & SHF_EXECINSTR)\n            && !strcmp(s->name, \".text\"))\n            text = s;\n        else if (s->sh_type == SHT_PROGBITS && s->data_offset > 0\n            && (!strcmp(s->name, \".rodata\") || !strcmp(s->name, \".data.ro\")))\n            rodata = s;\n    }\n    if (!text || text->data_offset == 0) {\n        tcc_error_noabort(\"ppc-macho: empty or missing .text section\");\n        return -1;\n    }\n    main_off = exe_find_main_offset(s1, text);\n    if (main_off < 0) {\n        tcc_error_noabort(\"ppc-macho: _main not defined in .text\");\n        return -1;\n    }\n\n    /* ---- Collect external function calls. ---- */\n    nstubs = collect_extern_stubs(s1, &stubs, &stub_for_elfsym);\n\n    /* ---- Plan section layout. ---- */\n    n_text_sects = 1;       /* __text */\n    if (rodata)\n        n_text_sects++;     /* __const */\n    if (nstubs > 0)\n        n_text_sects++;     /* __picsymbolstub1 (well, plain stub) */\n    n_data_sects = 0;\n    if (nstubs > 0)\n        n_data_sects++;     /* __nl_symbol_ptr */\n\n    total_msects = n_text_sects + n_data_sects;\n\n    /* ---- LC sizes -> hdr_and_lc_size. ---- */\n    text_seg_cmd_size = 56 + 68u * n_text_sects;\n    if (n_data_sects > 0)\n        data_seg_cmd_size = 56 + 68u * n_data_sects;\n\n    ncmds = 4;  /* PAGEZERO, TEXT, LINKEDIT, UNIXTHREAD */\n    total_cmds_size = pagezero_cmd_size + text_seg_cmd_size\n                    + linkedit_cmd_size + symtab_cmd_size\n                    + unixthread_cmd_size;\n    if (n_data_sects > 0) {\n        ncmds++;\n        total_cmds_size += data_seg_cmd_size;\n    }\n    /* LC_SYMTAB is included in total_cmds_size above and ncmds+1 below. */\n    ncmds++;\n    if (nstubs > 0) {\n        ncmds += 3;  /* LC_LOAD_DYLINKER, LC_LOAD_DYLIB, LC_DYSYMTAB */\n        total_cmds_size += dyld_cmd_size + dylib_cmd_size + dysymtab_cmd_size;\n    }\n    hdr_and_lc_size = 28 + total_cmds_size;\n\n    /* ---- Layout sections within __TEXT. ---- */\n    text_sect_vmaddr = text_seg_vmaddr + hdr_and_lc_size;\n    text_data_size = text->data_offset + 16;  /* + crt-shim */\n    cur_va = text_sect_vmaddr + text_data_size;\n    cur_off = hdr_and_lc_size + text_data_size;\n\n    sect_idx_text = nsec;\n    sects[nsec].elf = text;\n    sects[nsec].vmaddr = text_sect_vmaddr;\n    sects[nsec].size = text_data_size;\n    sects[nsec].file_off = hdr_and_lc_size;\n    sects[nsec].is_zerofill = 0;\n    nsec++;\n\n    if (rodata) {\n        cur_va = (cur_va + 3u) & ~3u;\n        cur_off = (cur_off + 3u) & ~3u;\n        sect_idx_rodata = nsec;\n        sects[nsec].elf = rodata;\n        sects[nsec].vmaddr = cur_va;\n        sects[nsec].size = rodata->data_offset;\n        sects[nsec].file_off = cur_off;\n        sects[nsec].is_zerofill = 0;\n        nsec++;\n        cur_va += rodata->data_offset;\n        cur_off += rodata->data_offset;\n    }\n\n    if (nstubs > 0) {\n        /* Stubs are 16 bytes each (4 instructions). Align to 16. */\n        cur_va  = (cur_va  + 15u) & ~15u;\n        cur_off = (cur_off + 15u) & ~15u;\n        sect_idx_stub = nsec;\n        sects[nsec].elf = NULL;\n        sects[nsec].vmaddr = cur_va;\n        sects[nsec].size = nstubs * 16u;\n        sects[nsec].file_off = cur_off;\n        sects[nsec].is_zerofill = 0;\n        nsec++;\n        cur_va  += nstubs * 16u;\n        cur_off += nstubs * 16u;\n    }\n\n    /* Round up __TEXT segment to page. */\n    text_seg_filesize = (cur_off + EXE_PAGE_SIZE - 1u) & ~(uint32_t)(EXE_PAGE_SIZE - 1);\n    text_seg_vmsize   = text_seg_filesize;\n\n    /* ---- Layout __DATA. ---- */\n    if (n_data_sects > 0) {\n        data_seg_vmaddr   = text_seg_vmaddr + text_seg_filesize;\n        data_seg_file_off = text_seg_filesize;\n\n        sect_idx_nlptr = nsec;\n        sects[nsec].elf = NULL;\n        sects[nsec].vmaddr = data_seg_vmaddr;\n        sects[nsec].size = nstubs * 4u;\n        sects[nsec].file_off = data_seg_file_off;\n        sects[nsec].is_zerofill = 0;\n        nsec++;\n\n        data_seg_filesize = (nstubs * 4u + EXE_PAGE_SIZE - 1u)\n                          & ~(uint32_t)(EXE_PAGE_SIZE - 1);\n    }\n\n    /* ---- Layout __LINKEDIT. ---- */\n    linkedit_file_off = text_seg_filesize + data_seg_filesize;\n    linkedit_vmaddr   = text_seg_vmaddr + text_seg_filesize + data_seg_filesize;\n\n    /* Now we know the stub addresses; set them in `stubs[]`. */\n    if (nstubs > 0) {\n        for (i = 0; i < nstubs; i++) {\n            stubs[i].addr = sects[sect_idx_stub].vmaddr + i * 16u;\n            stubs[i].la_ptr_addr = sects[sect_idx_nlptr].vmaddr + i * 4u;\n        }\n    }\n\n    /* ---- Allocate writable copies of section data. ---- */\n    new_text_size = text->data_offset;\n    text_data = tcc_malloc(new_text_size + 16);\n    memcpy(text_data, text->data, new_text_size);\n    memset(text_data + new_text_size, 0, 16);\n\n    if (rodata) {\n        rodata_data = tcc_malloc(rodata->data_offset);\n        memcpy(rodata_data, rodata->data, rodata->data_offset);\n    }\n\n    /* ---- Resolve relocations in text and rodata. ---- */\n    if (exe_resolve_section_relocs(s1, text, text_sect_vmaddr,\n                                    text_data, sects, nsec,\n                                    stubs, nstubs, stub_for_elfsym) < 0)\n        goto cleanup;\n    if (rodata && exe_resolve_section_relocs(s1, rodata,\n                                              sects[sect_idx_rodata].vmaddr,\n                                              rodata_data, sects, nsec,\n                                              stubs, nstubs,\n                                              stub_for_elfsym) < 0)\n        goto cleanup;\n\n    /* ---- Append crt-shim. ---- */\n    shim_off = exe_emit_crt_shim(s1, text_data, &new_text_size, main_off);\n    if (shim_off < 0)\n        goto cleanup;\n    /* text_data_size already accounts for the +16 shim; size is fixed. */\n    entry_addr = text_sect_vmaddr + (uint32_t)shim_off;\n\n    /* ---- Generate stub bytes (4 instructions per stub):\n     *      lis   r12, ha(slot)\n     *      lwz   r12, lo(slot)(r12)\n     *      mtctr r12\n     *      bctr\n     */\n    if (nstubs > 0) {\n        stub_data = tcc_mallocz(nstubs * 16);\n        nl_ptr_data = tcc_mallocz(nstubs * 4);  /* zero-filled; dyld writes */\n        for (i = 0; i < nstubs; i++) {\n            uint32_t slot = stubs[i].la_ptr_addr;\n            uint16_t ha = (uint16_t)((slot + 0x8000) >> 16);\n            uint16_t lo = (uint16_t)(slot & 0xffff);\n            uint32_t insns[4];\n            int j;\n            insns[0] = 0x3d800000u | ha;        /* lis r12, ha */\n            insns[1] = 0x818c0000u | lo;        /* lwz r12, lo(r12) */\n            insns[2] = 0x7d8903a6u;             /* mtctr r12 */\n            insns[3] = 0x4e800420u;             /* bctr */\n            for (j = 0; j < 4; j++) {\n                stub_data[i*16 + j*4 + 0] = (insns[j] >> 24) & 0xff;\n                stub_data[i*16 + j*4 + 1] = (insns[j] >> 16) & 0xff;\n                stub_data[i*16 + j*4 + 2] = (insns[j] >>  8) & 0xff;\n                stub_data[i*16 + j*4 + 3] =  insns[j]        & 0xff;\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    /* ---- Build symbol table.\n     *\n     * Three groups required by Mach-O DYSYMTAB:\n     *   1. local syms (defined, non-external)\n     *   2. extdef syms (defined, external) \u2014 _main, _start\n     *   3. undef syms \u2014 one per stub (e.g. _printf)\n     *\n     * We only emit the canonical entries: _main, _start, and one undef\n     * per stub. Local syms not strictly required for an executable. */\n    obuf_put(&strtab, \"\", 1);\n\n    /* No locals in our minimal symtab. */\n    n_localsym = 0;\n\n    /* Externally-defined: _main, _start. */\n    {\n        uint32_t main_strx = (uint32_t)strtab.len;\n        obuf_put(&strtab, \"_main\", 6);\n        put_nlist(&nlist, main_strx, N_SECT | N_EXT, SECT_TEXT, 0,\n                  text_sect_vmaddr + main_off);\n        n_extdefsym++;\n    }\n    {\n        uint32_t start_strx = (uint32_t)strtab.len;\n        obuf_put(&strtab, \"_start\", 7);\n        put_nlist(&nlist, start_strx, N_SECT | N_EXT, SECT_TEXT, 0,\n                  entry_addr);\n        n_extdefsym++;\n    }\n\n    /* Undef externals: one per stub. */\n    if (nstubs > 0) {\n        stub_sym_idx = tcc_mallocz(nstubs * sizeof(int));\n        for (i = 0; i < nstubs; i++) {\n            ElfW(Sym) *esym = (ElfW(Sym) *)s1->symtab->data + stubs[i].elfsym_idx;\n            const char *name = (char *)s1->symtab->link->data + esym->st_name;\n            uint32_t strx = (uint32_t)strtab.len;\n            obuf_put(&strtab, name, strlen(name) + 1);\n            stub_sym_idx[i] = n_localsym + n_extdefsym + n_undefsym;\n            put_nlist(&nlist, strx, N_EXT | N_UNDF, NO_SECT, 0, 0);\n            n_undefsym++;\n        }\n    }\n\n    /* ---- Build indirect symbol table.\n     *\n     * One entry per __nl_symbol_ptr slot: the symbol index of the\n     * corresponding undef-external. dyld uses this to know which\n     * symbol to bind the slot to. (For non-lazy ptrs, dyld resolves\n     * at load time.) */\n    if (nstubs > 0) {\n        for (i = 0; i < nstubs; i++)\n            put32be(&indirect, stub_sym_idx[i]);\n    }\n\n    /* ---- Compute __LINKEDIT layout. ---- */\n    {\n        uint32_t loff = linkedit_file_off;\n        if (nstubs > 0) {\n            indirect_file_off = loff;\n            loff += (uint32_t)indirect.len;\n        }\n        sym_file_off = loff;\n        loff += (uint32_t)nlist.len;\n        str_file_off = loff;\n        loff += (uint32_t)strtab.len;\n        linkedit_filesize = loff - linkedit_file_off;\n    }\n\n    /* ---- Serialize: Mach header. ---- */\n    put32be(&out, MH_MAGIC);\n    put32be(&out, CPU_TYPE_POWERPC);\n    put32be(&out, CPU_SUBTYPE_POWERPC_ALL);\n    put32be(&out, MH_EXECUTE);\n    put32be(&out, ncmds);\n    put32be(&out, total_cmds_size);\n    put32be(&out, (nstubs > 0) ? (MH_DYLDLINK | MH_TWOLEVEL) : MH_NOUNDEFS);\n\n    /* ---- LC_SEGMENT __PAGEZERO. ---- */\n    put32be(&out, LC_SEGMENT);\n    put32be(&out, pagezero_cmd_size);\n    put_sectname(&out, \"__PAGEZERO\");\n    put32be(&out, 0);\n    put32be(&out, EXE_PAGEZERO_VMSIZE);\n    put32be(&out, 0);\n    put32be(&out, 0);\n    put32be(&out, 0);\n    put32be(&out, 0);\n    put32be(&out, 0);\n    put32be(&out, 0);\n\n    /* ---- LC_SEGMENT __TEXT. ---- */\n    put32be(&out, LC_SEGMENT);\n    put32be(&out, text_seg_cmd_size);\n    put_sectname(&out, \"__TEXT\");\n    put32be(&out, text_seg_vmaddr);\n    put32be(&out, text_seg_vmsize);\n    put32be(&out, 0);\n    put32be(&out, text_seg_filesize);\n    put32be(&out, VM_PROT_READ | VM_PROT_WRITE | VM_PROT_EXECUTE);\n    put32be(&out, VM_PROT_READ | VM_PROT_EXECUTE);\n    put32be(&out, n_text_sects);\n    put32be(&out, 0);\n    /* __text section header */\n    put_sectname(&out, \"__text\");\n    put_sectname(&out, \"__TEXT\");\n    put32be(&out, sects[sect_idx_text].vmaddr);\n    put32be(&out, sects[sect_idx_text].size);\n    put32be(&out, sects[sect_idx_text].file_off);\n    put32be(&out, 2);   /* align 2^2 */\n    put32be(&out, 0);\n    put32be(&out, 0);\n    put32be(&out, S_REGULAR | S_ATTR_PURE_INSTRUCTIONS | S_ATTR_SOME_INSTRUCTIONS);\n    put32be(&out, 0);\n    put32be(&out, 0);\n    /* __const if present */\n    if (rodata) {\n        put_sectname(&out, \"__const\");\n        put_sectname(&out, \"__TEXT\");\n        put32be(&out, sects[sect_idx_rodata].vmaddr);\n        put32be(&out, sects[sect_idx_rodata].size);\n        put32be(&out, sects[sect_idx_rodata].file_off);\n        put32be(&out, 2);\n        put32be(&out, 0);\n        put32be(&out, 0);\n        put32be(&out, S_REGULAR);\n        put32be(&out, 0);\n        put32be(&out, 0);\n    }\n    /* stub section if present */\n    if (nstubs > 0) {\n        put_sectname(&out, \"__symbol_stub\");\n        put_sectname(&out, \"__TEXT\");\n        put32be(&out, sects[sect_idx_stub].vmaddr);\n        put32be(&out, sects[sect_idx_stub].size);\n        put32be(&out, sects[sect_idx_stub].file_off);\n        put32be(&out, 4);     /* align 2^4 = 16 */\n        put32be(&out, 0);\n        put32be(&out, 0);\n        put32be(&out, S_SYMBOL_STUBS | S_ATTR_PURE_INSTRUCTIONS\n                       | S_ATTR_SOME_INSTRUCTIONS);\n        put32be(&out, 0);     /* reserved1: indirect symtab base index */\n        put32be(&out, 16);    /* reserved2: stub size */\n    }\n\n    /* ---- LC_SEGMENT __DATA. ---- */\n    if (n_data_sects > 0) {\n        put32be(&out, LC_SEGMENT);\n        put32be(&out, data_seg_cmd_size);\n        put_sectname(&out, \"__DATA\");\n        put32be(&out, data_seg_vmaddr);\n        put32be(&out, data_seg_filesize);\n        put32be(&out, data_seg_file_off);\n        put32be(&out, data_seg_filesize);\n        put32be(&out, VM_PROT_READ | VM_PROT_WRITE | VM_PROT_EXECUTE);\n        put32be(&out, VM_PROT_READ | VM_PROT_WRITE);\n        put32be(&out, n_data_sects);\n        put32be(&out, 0);\n        /* __nl_symbol_ptr */\n        put_sectname(&out, \"__nl_symbol_ptr\");\n        put_sectname(&out, \"__DATA\");\n        put32be(&out, sects[sect_idx_nlptr].vmaddr);\n        put32be(&out, sects[sect_idx_nlptr].size);\n        put32be(&out, sects[sect_idx_nlptr].file_off);\n        put32be(&out, 2);\n        put32be(&out, 0);\n        put32be(&out, 0);\n        put32be(&out, S_NON_LAZY_SYMBOL_POINTERS);\n        put32be(&out, 0);     /* reserved1: indirect symtab base index */\n        put32be(&out, 0);\n    }\n\n    /* ---- LC_SEGMENT __LINKEDIT. ---- */\n    put32be(&out, LC_SEGMENT);\n    put32be(&out, linkedit_cmd_size);\n    put_sectname(&out, \"__LINKEDIT\");\n    put32be(&out, linkedit_vmaddr);\n    put32be(&out, (linkedit_filesize + EXE_PAGE_SIZE - 1u)\n                  & ~(uint32_t)(EXE_PAGE_SIZE - 1));\n    put32be(&out, linkedit_file_off);\n    put32be(&out, linkedit_filesize);\n    put32be(&out, VM_PROT_READ | VM_PROT_WRITE | VM_PROT_EXECUTE);\n    put32be(&out, VM_PROT_READ);\n    put32be(&out, 0);\n    put32be(&out, 0);\n\n    /* ---- LC_LOAD_DYLINKER (only if we use libSystem). ---- */\n    if (nstubs > 0) {\n        uint32_t k;\n        put32be(&out, LC_LOAD_DYLINKER);\n        put32be(&out, dyld_cmd_size);\n        put32be(&out, 12);    /* offset of name in this lc */\n        obuf_put(&out, dyld_path, sizeof(dyld_path));\n        for (k = sizeof(dyld_path); k < dyld_path_aligned; k++)\n            put8(&out, 0);\n    }\n\n    /* ---- LC_LOAD_DYLIB libSystem. ---- */\n    if (nstubs > 0) {\n        uint32_t k;\n        put32be(&out, LC_LOAD_DYLIB);\n        put32be(&out, dylib_cmd_size);\n        put32be(&out, 24);    /* name offset */\n        put32be(&out, 0);     /* timestamp */\n        put32be(&out, 0x00580000);   /* current_version (88.0.0) */\n        put32be(&out, 0x00010000);   /* compat_version  (1.0.0) */\n        obuf_put(&out, dylib_path, sizeof(dylib_path));\n        for (k = sizeof(dylib_path); k < dylib_path_aligned; k++)\n            put8(&out, 0);\n    }\n\n    /* ---- LC_SYMTAB. ---- */\n    put32be(&out, LC_SYMTAB);\n    put32be(&out, symtab_cmd_size);\n    put32be(&out, sym_file_off);\n    put32be(&out, n_localsym + n_extdefsym + n_undefsym);\n    put32be(&out, str_file_off);\n    put32be(&out, (uint32_t)strtab.len);\n\n    /* ---- LC_DYSYMTAB. ---- */\n    if (nstubs > 0) {\n        put32be(&out, LC_DYSYMTAB);\n        put32be(&out, dysymtab_cmd_size);\n        put32be(&out, 0);                            /* ilocalsym */\n        put32be(&out, n_localsym);                   /* nlocalsym */\n        put32be(&out, n_localsym);                   /* iextdefsym */\n        put32be(&out, n_extdefsym);                  /* nextdefsym */\n        put32be(&out, n_localsym + n_extdefsym);     /* iundefsym */\n        put32be(&out, n_undefsym);                   /* nundefsym */\n        put32be(&out, 0);                            /* tocoff */\n        put32be(&out, 0);                            /* ntoc */\n        put32be(&out, 0);                            /* modtaboff */\n        put32be(&out, 0);                            /* nmodtab */\n        put32be(&out, 0);                            /* extrefsymoff */\n        put32be(&out, 0);                            /* nextrefsyms */\n        put32be(&out, indirect_file_off);            /* indirectsymoff */\n        put32be(&out, nstubs);                       /* nindirectsyms */\n        put32be(&out, 0);                            /* extreloff */\n        put32be(&out, 0);                            /* nextrel */\n        put32be(&out, 0);                            /* locreloff */\n        put32be(&out, 0);                            /* nlocrel */\n    }\n\n    /* ---- LC_UNIXTHREAD with PPC_THREAD_STATE. ---- */\n    put32be(&out, LC_UNIXTHREAD);\n    put32be(&out, unixthread_cmd_size);\n    put32be(&out, PPC_THREAD_STATE);\n    put32be(&out, PPC_THREAD_STATE_COUNT);\n    {\n        int wi;\n        for (wi = 0; wi < PPC_THREAD_STATE_COUNT; wi++) {\n            uint32_t v = 0;\n            if (wi == 0)\n                v = entry_addr;       /* srr0 = entry PC */\n            put32be(&out, v);\n        }\n    }\n\n    /* ---- Pad to text data offset, then write text. ---- */\n    while (out.len < hdr_and_lc_size)\n        put8(&out, 0);\n    obuf_put(&out, text_data, text_data_size);\n\n    /* ---- rodata. ---- */\n    if (rodata) {\n        while (out.len < sects[sect_idx_rodata].file_off)\n            put8(&out, 0);\n        obuf_put(&out, rodata_data, sects[sect_idx_rodata].size);\n    }\n\n    /* ---- stubs. ---- */\n    if (nstubs > 0) {\n        while (out.len < sects[sect_idx_stub].file_off)\n            put8(&out, 0);\n        obuf_put(&out, stub_data, sects[sect_idx_stub].size);\n    }\n\n    /* ---- Pad to __DATA boundary. ---- */\n    if (n_data_sects > 0) {\n        while (out.len < data_seg_file_off)\n            put8(&out, 0);\n        obuf_put(&out, nl_ptr_data, sects[sect_idx_nlptr].size);\n    }\n\n    /* ---- Pad to __LINKEDIT, then indirect/sym/strtab. ---- */\n    while (out.len < linkedit_file_off)\n        put8(&out, 0);\n    if (nstubs > 0) {\n        while (out.len < indirect_file_off)\n            put8(&out, 0);\n        obuf_put(&out, indirect.buf, indirect.len);\n    }\n    while (out.len < sym_file_off)\n        put8(&out, 0);\n    obuf_put(&out, nlist.buf, nlist.len);\n    while (out.len < str_file_off)\n        put8(&out, 0);\n    obuf_put(&out, strtab.buf, strtab.len);\n\n    /* ---- Write file. ---- */\n    unlink(filename);\n    fd = open(filename, O_WRONLY | O_CREAT | O_TRUNC | O_BINARY, 0755);\n    if (fd < 0 || (fp = fdopen(fd, \"wb\")) == NULL) {\n        tcc_error_noabort(\"could not write '%s': %s\", filename, strerror(errno));\n        if (fd >= 0) close(fd);\n        goto cleanup;\n    }\n    if (fwrite(out.buf, 1, out.len, fp) != out.len) {\n        tcc_error_noabort(\"short write to '%s'\", filename);\n        goto cleanup;\n    }\n    fclose(fp); fp = NULL;\n    chmod(filename, 0755);\n    if (s1->verbose)\n        printf(\"<- %s (exe %u bytes, entry 0x%x, %d stubs)\\n\",\n               filename, (unsigned)out.len, entry_addr, nstubs);\n    ret = 0;\n\ncleanup:\n    if (fp) fclose(fp);\n    tcc_free(out.buf);\n    tcc_free(nlist.buf);\n    tcc_free(strtab.buf);\n    tcc_free(indirect.buf);\n    tcc_free(text_data);\n    tcc_free(rodata_data);\n    tcc_free(stub_data);\n    tcc_free(nl_ptr_data);\n    tcc_free(stubs);\n    tcc_free(stub_for_elfsym);\n    tcc_free(stub_sym_idx);\n    return ret;\n}\n\n"
}

## 🤖 Claude

## 🤖 Claude

Need to set the library ordinal in n_desc for two-level namespace lookup:

---

## 🤖 Claude


🔧 Using tool: Edit
Input: {
  "replace_all": false,
  "file_path": "/Users/cell/claude/tcc-darwin8-ppc/tcc/ppc-macho.c",
  "old_string": "    /* Undef externals: one per stub. */\n    if (nstubs > 0) {\n        stub_sym_idx = tcc_mallocz(nstubs * sizeof(int));\n        for (i = 0; i < nstubs; i++) {\n            ElfW(Sym) *esym = (ElfW(Sym) *)s1->symtab->data + stubs[i].elfsym_idx;\n            const char *name = (char *)s1->symtab->link->data + esym->st_name;\n            uint32_t strx = (uint32_t)strtab.len;\n            obuf_put(&strtab, name, strlen(name) + 1);\n            stub_sym_idx[i] = n_localsym + n_extdefsym + n_undefsym;\n            put_nlist(&nlist, strx, N_EXT | N_UNDF, NO_SECT, 0, 0);\n            n_undefsym++;\n        }\n    }",
  "new_string": "    /* Undef externals: one per stub. n_desc carries the two-level\n     * namespace library ordinal in its high byte; ord 1 = first\n     * LC_LOAD_DYLIB (libSystem in our case). Without this dyld will\n     * look for the symbol in our own executable and fail. */\n    if (nstubs > 0) {\n        stub_sym_idx = tcc_mallocz(nstubs * sizeof(int));\n        for (i = 0; i < nstubs; i++) {\n            ElfW(Sym) *esym = (ElfW(Sym) *)s1->symtab->data + stubs[i].elfsym_idx;\n            const char *name = (char *)s1->symtab->link->data + esym->st_name;\n            uint32_t strx = (uint32_t)strtab.len;\n            uint16_t n_desc = (1u << 8);   /* LIBRARY_ORDINAL = 1 */\n            obuf_put(&strtab, name, strlen(name) + 1);\n            stub_sym_idx[i] = n_localsym + n_extdefsym + n_undefsym;\n            put_nlist(&nlist, strx, N_EXT | N_UNDF, NO_SECT, n_desc, 0);\n            n_undefsym++;\n        }\n    }"
}

## 🤖 Claude

Let me simplify: the stubs section doesn't need to be S_SYMBOL_STUBS since they're just loader code. Mark it S_REGULAR:

---

## 🤖 Claude


🔧 Using tool: Edit
Input: {
  "replace_all": false,
  "file_path": "/Users/cell/claude/tcc-darwin8-ppc/tcc/ppc-macho.c",
  "old_string": "    /* stub section if present */\n    if (nstubs > 0) {\n        put_sectname(&out, \"__symbol_stub\");\n        put_sectname(&out, \"__TEXT\");\n        put32be(&out, sects[sect_idx_stub].vmaddr);\n        put32be(&out, sects[sect_idx_stub].size);\n        put32be(&out, sects[sect_idx_stub].file_off);\n        put32be(&out, 4);     /* align 2^4 = 16 */\n        put32be(&out, 0);\n        put32be(&out, 0);\n        put32be(&out, S_SYMBOL_STUBS | S_ATTR_PURE_INSTRUCTIONS\n                       | S_ATTR_SOME_INSTRUCTIONS);\n        put32be(&out, 0);     /* reserved1: indirect symtab base index */\n        put32be(&out, 16);    /* reserved2: stub size */\n    }",
  "new_string": "    /* stub section if present. We use plain S_REGULAR (not\n     * S_SYMBOL_STUBS) because our stubs are simple PIC-free loader\n     * code that reads from the __nl_symbol_ptr slot \u2014 they don't\n     * need binding themselves; only the slots do. That keeps the\n     * indirect symbol table to N entries (one per nl_ptr). */\n    if (nstubs > 0) {\n        put_sectname(&out, \"__symbol_stub\");\n        put_sectname(&out, \"__TEXT\");\n        put32be(&out, sects[sect_idx_stub].vmaddr);\n        put32be(&out, sects[sect_idx_stub].size);\n        put32be(&out, sects[sect_idx_stub].file_off);\n        put32be(&out, 4);     /* align 2^4 = 16 */\n        put32be(&out, 0);\n        put32be(&out, 0);\n        put32be(&out, S_REGULAR | S_ATTR_PURE_INSTRUCTIONS\n                       | S_ATTR_SOME_INSTRUCTIONS);\n        put32be(&out, 0);\n        put32be(&out, 0);\n    }"
}

## 🤖 Claude

The crash is likely due to no stack setup before calling main. Let me fix the crt-shim:

---

## 🤖 Claude


🔧 Using tool: Bash
Input: {
  "command": "grep -n \"exe_emit_crt_shim\\b\" tcc/ppc-macho.c | head",
  "description": "Find crt-shim"
}


---

## 🤖 Claude


🔧 Using tool: Read
Input: {
  "file_path": "/Users/cell/claude/tcc-darwin8-ppc/tcc/ppc-macho.c",
  "offset": 1129,
  "limit": 45
}


---

## 🤖 Claude


🔧 Using tool: Edit
Input: {
  "replace_all": false,
  "file_path": "/Users/cell/claude/tcc-darwin8-ppc/tcc/ppc-macho.c",
  "old_string": "/* Helper: emit the crt-shim into `text` after the user code. The shim:\n *   bl   _main            (REL24, displacement = main_off - shim_off)\n *   li   r0, 1            (SYS_exit)\n *   sc\n *   trap                  (defensive)\n * Returns the shim's offset within the section. */\nstatic int exe_emit_crt_shim(TCCState *s1, unsigned char *text_data,\n                             int *text_size, int main_off)\n{\n    int shim_off = *text_size;\n    int disp;\n    uint32_t bl_word;\n    uint32_t i;\n\n    disp = main_off - shim_off;\n    if (disp < -(1 << 25) || disp >= (1 << 25)) {\n        tcc_error_noabort(\"ppc-macho: crt shim too far from main (disp=0x%x)\",\n                          (unsigned)disp);\n        return -1;\n    }\n    bl_word = 0x48000001u | ((uint32_t)disp & 0x03fffffc);\n\n    i = shim_off;\n    text_data[i+0] = (bl_word >> 24) & 0xff;\n    text_data[i+1] = (bl_word >> 16) & 0xff;\n    text_data[i+2] = (bl_word >>  8) & 0xff;\n    text_data[i+3] =  bl_word        & 0xff;\n    text_data[i+4] = 0x38; text_data[i+5] = 0x00;\n    text_data[i+6] = 0x00; text_data[i+7] = 0x01;\n    text_data[i+8] = 0x44; text_data[i+9] = 0x00;\n    text_data[i+10]= 0x00; text_data[i+11]= 0x02;\n    text_data[i+12]= 0x7f; text_data[i+13]= 0xe0;\n    text_data[i+14]= 0x00; text_data[i+15]= 0x08;\n\n    *text_size = shim_off + 16;\n    return shim_off;\n}",
  "new_string": "/* Helper: emit the crt-shim into `text` after the user code. The shim\n * sets up a minimal stack frame, calls _main, then exits via SYS_exit.\n *\n * Apple's crt1 also sets up argc/argv/envp and aligns the stack, but\n * for simple (non-argv-using) programs none of that is required as\n * long as the called function has room to push its own frame. The\n * kernel hands us a usable r1 already (it points at argc on the stack)\n * \u2014 we just push a small frame to give main its own LR slot.\n *\n * Layout (12 instructions, 48 bytes):\n *   stwu r1, -64(r1)         ; reserve a 64-byte frame\n *   mflr r0                  ; (LR is whatever the kernel left)\n *   stw  r0, 72(r1)          ; save LR in caller's slot of next frame\n *   bl   _main               ; r3 = main's return value\n *   li   r0, 1               ; SYS_exit\n *   sc                       ; trap to kernel; never returns\n *   trap                     ; defensive\n */\nstatic int exe_emit_crt_shim(TCCState *s1, unsigned char *text_data,\n                             int *text_size, int main_off)\n{\n    int shim_off = *text_size;\n    int disp;\n    uint32_t bl_word;\n    uint32_t i;\n    int bl_off;\n\n    /* The bl is the 4th instruction of the shim. */\n    bl_off = shim_off + 12;\n    disp = main_off - bl_off;\n    if (disp < -(1 << 25) || disp >= (1 << 25)) {\n        tcc_error_noabort(\"ppc-macho: crt shim too far from main (disp=0x%x)\",\n                          (unsigned)disp);\n        return -1;\n    }\n    bl_word = 0x48000001u | ((uint32_t)disp & 0x03fffffc);\n\n    i = shim_off;\n    /* stwu r1, -64(r1)  ->  0x9421ffc0 */\n    text_data[i+0] = 0x94; text_data[i+1] = 0x21;\n    text_data[i+2] = 0xff; text_data[i+3] = 0xc0;\n    /* mflr r0  ->  0x7c0802a6 */\n    text_data[i+4] = 0x7c; text_data[i+5] = 0x08;\n    text_data[i+6] = 0x02; text_data[i+7] = 0xa6;\n    /* stw r0, 72(r1)  ->  0x90010048  (caller's LR slot at SP+8 of new frame\n       is at our_SP+72 since new SP is old_SP-64; old_SP+8 = new_SP+72) */\n    text_data[i+8]  = 0x90; text_data[i+9]  = 0x01;\n    text_data[i+10] = 0x00; text_data[i+11] = 0x48;\n    /* bl _main */\n    text_data[i+12] = (bl_word >> 24) & 0xff;\n    text_data[i+13] = (bl_word >> 16) & 0xff;\n    text_data[i+14] = (bl_word >>  8) & 0xff;\n    text_data[i+15] =  bl_word        & 0xff;\n    /* li r0, 1 */\n    text_data[i+16] = 0x38; text_data[i+17] = 0x00;\n    text_data[i+18] = 0x00; text_data[i+19] = 0x01;\n    /* sc */\n    text_data[i+20] = 0x44; text_data[i+21] = 0x00;\n    text_data[i+22] = 0x00; text_data[i+23] = 0x02;\n    /* trap */\n    text_data[i+24] = 0x7f; text_data[i+25] = 0xe0;\n    text_data[i+26] = 0x00; text_data[i+27] = 0x08;\n\n    *text_size = shim_off + 28;\n    return shim_off;\n}"
}

  "new_string": "    text_data = tcc_malloc(new_text_size + 32);\n    memcpy(text_data, text->data, new_text_size);\n    memset(text_data + new_text_size, 0, 32);"

## 🤖 Claude


🔧 Using tool: Edit
Input: {
  "replace_all": false,
  "file_path": "/Users/cell/claude/tcc-darwin8-ppc/tcc/ppc-macho.c",
  "old_string": "    text_data_size = text->data_offset + 32;  /* + crt-shim (28B padded) */",
  "new_string": "    /* Reserve room for the crt-shim (28 bytes); pad up to 4-byte\n     * alignment for the next section's start. */\n    text_data_size = (text->data_offset + 28u + 3u) & ~3u;"

## 🤖 Claude

The stack might not be aligned. Let me update the crt-shim to align it properly:

---

## 🤖 Claude


🔧 Using tool: Edit
Input: {
  "replace_all": false,
  "file_path": "/Users/cell/claude/tcc-darwin8-ppc/tcc/ppc-macho.c",
  "old_string": "/* Helper: emit the crt-shim into `text` after the user code. The shim\n * sets up a minimal stack frame, calls _main, then exits via SYS_exit.\n *\n * Apple's crt1 also sets up argc/argv/envp and aligns the stack, but\n * for simple (non-argv-using) programs none of that is required as\n * long as the called function has room to push its own frame. The\n * kernel hands us a usable r1 already (it points at argc on the stack)\n * \u2014 we just push a small frame to give main its own LR slot.\n *\n * Layout (12 instructions, 48 bytes):\n *   stwu r1, -64(r1)         ; reserve a 64-byte frame\n *   mflr r0                  ; (LR is whatever the kernel left)\n *   stw  r0, 72(r1)          ; save LR in caller's slot of next frame\n *   bl   _main               ; r3 = main's return value\n *   li   r0, 1               ; SYS_exit\n *   sc                       ; trap to kernel; never returns\n *   trap                     ; defensive\n */\nstatic int exe_emit_crt_shim(TCCState *s1, unsigned char *text_data,\n                             int *text_size, int main_off)\n{\n    int shim_off = *text_size;\n    int disp;\n    uint32_t bl_word;\n    uint32_t i;\n    int bl_off;\n\n    /* The bl is the 4th instruction of the shim. */\n    bl_off = shim_off + 12;\n    disp = main_off - bl_off;\n    if (disp < -(1 << 25) || disp >= (1 << 25)) {\n        tcc_error_noabort(\"ppc-macho: crt shim too far from main (disp=0x%x)\",\n                          (unsigned)disp);\n        return -1;\n    }\n    bl_word = 0x48000001u | ((uint32_t)disp & 0x03fffffc);\n\n    i = shim_off;\n    /* stwu r1, -64(r1)  ->  0x9421ffc0 */\n    text_data[i+0] = 0x94; text_data[i+1] = 0x21;\n    text_data[i+2] = 0xff; text_data[i+3] = 0xc0;\n    /* mflr r0  ->  0x7c0802a6 */\n    text_data[i+4] = 0x7c; text_data[i+5] = 0x08;\n    text_data[i+6] = 0x02; text_data[i+7] = 0xa6;\n    /* stw r0, 72(r1)  ->  0x90010048  (caller's LR slot at SP+8 of new frame\n       is at our_SP+72 since new SP is old_SP-64; old_SP+8 = new_SP+72) */\n    text_data[i+8]  = 0x90; text_data[i+9]  = 0x01;\n    text_data[i+10] = 0x00; text_data[i+11] = 0x48;\n    /* bl _main */\n    text_data[i+12] = (bl_word >> 24) & 0xff;\n    text_data[i+13] = (bl_word >> 16) & 0xff;\n    text_data[i+14] = (bl_word >>  8) & 0xff;\n    text_data[i+15] =  bl_word        & 0xff;\n    /* li r0, 1 */\n    text_data[i+16] = 0x38; text_data[i+17] = 0x00;\n    text_data[i+18] = 0x00; text_data[i+19] = 0x01;\n    /* sc */\n    text_data[i+20] = 0x44; text_data[i+21] = 0x00;\n    text_data[i+22] = 0x00; text_data[i+23] = 0x02;\n    /* trap */\n    text_data[i+24] = 0x7f; text_data[i+25] = 0xe0;\n    text_data[i+26] = 0x00; text_data[i+27] = 0x08;\n\n    *text_size = shim_off + 28;\n    return shim_off;\n}",
  "new_string": "/* Helper: emit the crt-shim into `text` after the user code.\n *\n * Apple's crt1 (the standard /usr/lib/crt1.o) does considerably more\n * than this: it aligns r1 to a 32-byte boundary, parses argc/argv/envp\n * off the kernel stack and stashes them in libSystem globals\n * (_NXArgc, _NXArgv, _environ), and chains into __dyld_init to ensure\n * library initializers have run. We replicate the bare minimum:\n *\n *   addi r1, r1, -4           ; the kernel hands us r1 pointing at argc\n *   rlwinm r1, r1, 0, 0, 26   ; align r1 down to 32 bytes\n *   li   r0, 0\n *   stw  r0, 0(r1)            ; clear the back-chain word\n *   stwu r1, -64(r1)          ; reserve a small linkage frame\n *   bl   _main                ; r3 = main's return value\n *   li   r0, 1                ; SYS_exit\n *   sc\n *   trap                      ; defensive (sc doesn't return)\n *\n * 9 instructions, 36 bytes.\n */\nstatic int exe_emit_crt_shim(TCCState *s1, unsigned char *text_data,\n                             int *text_size, int main_off)\n{\n    int shim_off = *text_size;\n    int disp;\n    uint32_t bl_word;\n    uint32_t i;\n    int bl_off;\n\n    /* bl is the 6th instruction of the shim (offset 20). */\n    bl_off = shim_off + 20;\n    disp = main_off - bl_off;\n    if (disp < -(1 << 25) || disp >= (1 << 25)) {\n        tcc_error_noabort(\"ppc-macho: crt shim too far from main (disp=0x%x)\",\n                          (unsigned)disp);\n        return -1;\n    }\n    bl_word = 0x48000001u | ((uint32_t)disp & 0x03fffffc);\n\n    i = shim_off;\n    /* addi r1, r1, -4   ->  0x3821fffc */\n    text_data[i+0]  = 0x38; text_data[i+1]  = 0x21;\n    text_data[i+2]  = 0xff; text_data[i+3]  = 0xfc;\n    /* rlwinm r1, r1, 0, 0, 26  ->  0x5421003a\n       (rotate by 0, mask bits 0..26 = upper 27 bits = align to 32 bytes) */\n    text_data[i+4]  = 0x54; text_data[i+5]  = 0x21;\n    text_data[i+6]  = 0x00; text_data[i+7]  = 0x36;\n    /* li r0, 0  ->  0x38000000 */\n    text_data[i+8]  = 0x38; text_data[i+9]  = 0x00;\n    text_data[i+10] = 0x00; text_data[i+11] = 0x00;\n    /* stw r0, 0(r1)  ->  0x90010000 */\n    text_data[i+12] = 0x90; text_data[i+13] = 0x01;\n    text_data[i+14] = 0x00; text_data[i+15] = 0x00;\n    /* stwu r1, -64(r1)  ->  0x9421ffc0 */\n    text_data[i+16] = 0x94; text_data[i+17] = 0x21;\n    text_data[i+18] = 0xff; text_data[i+19] = 0xc0;\n    /* bl _main */\n    text_data[i+20] = (bl_word >> 24) & 0xff;\n    text_data[i+21] = (bl_word >> 16) & 0xff;\n    text_data[i+22] = (bl_word >>  8) & 0xff;\n    text_data[i+23] =  bl_word        & 0xff;\n    /* li r0, 1 */\n    text_data[i+24] = 0x38; text_data[i+25] = 0x00;\n    text_data[i+26] = 0x00; text_data[i+27] = 0x01;\n    /* sc */\n    text_data[i+28] = 0x44; text_data[i+29] = 0x00;\n    text_data[i+30] = 0x00; text_data[i+31] = 0x02;\n    /* trap */\n    text_data[i+32] = 0x7f; text_data[i+33] = 0xe0;\n    text_data[i+34] = 0x00; text_data[i+35] = 0x08;\n\n    *text_size = shim_off + 36;\n    return shim_off;\n}"
}

## 🤖 Claude

Bug: my rlwinm encoding has ME=27 instead of 26. Fix:

---

## 🤖 Claude


🔧 Using tool: Edit
Input: {
  "replace_all": false,
  "file_path": "/Users/cell/claude/tcc-darwin8-ppc/tcc/ppc-macho.c",
  "old_string": "    /* rlwinm r1, r1, 0, 0, 26  ->  0x5421003a\n       (rotate by 0, mask bits 0..26 = upper 27 bits = align to 32 bytes) */\n    text_data[i+4]  = 0x54; text_data[i+5]  = 0x21;\n    text_data[i+6]  = 0x00; text_data[i+7]  = 0x36;",
  "new_string": "    /* rlwinm r1, r1, 0, 0, 26  ->  0x54210034\n       (rotate by 0, mask bits 0..26 = clear bottom 5 bits = 32-byte align)\n       Encoding: opcode 21, rS=1, rA=1, SH=0, MB=0, ME=26, Rc=0:\n         0x54000000 | (1<<21) | (1<<16) | (0<<11) | (0<<6) | (26<<1) | 0\n         = 0x54210034 */\n    text_data[i+4]  = 0x54; text_data[i+5]  = 0x21;\n    text_data[i+6]  = 0x00; text_data[i+7]  = 0x34;"
}

My new function has 9 args (>8 GPR slots). Let me bundle them into a struct:

## 🤖 Claude


🔧 Using tool: Edit
Input: {
  "replace_all": false,
  "file_path": "/Users/cell/claude/tcc-darwin8-ppc/tcc/ppc-macho.c",
  "old_string": "/* Resolve relocations within `s` against the laid-out sections and\n * stubs. Modifies the section data buffer in place. Returns 0 on\n * success, -1 on error.\n *\n * `sect_data` is a writable copy of the section's bytes (modified\n * in place). `sect_vmaddr` is where this section will be loaded.\n */\nstatic int exe_resolve_section_relocs(TCCState *s1, Section *s,\n                                      uint32_t sect_vmaddr,\n                                      unsigned char *sect_data,\n                                      struct exe_sect *all_sects,\n                                      int nsec,\n                                      struct extern_stub *stubs,\n                                      int nstubs,\n                                      int *stub_for_elfsym)\n{",
  "new_string": "/* Bundled context for exe_resolve_section_relocs(); keeps the param\n * count to 5 so it stays within tcc's PPC backend's 8-GPR-slot\n * function call limit. */\nstruct exe_reloc_ctx {\n    struct exe_sect    *all_sects;\n    int                 nsec;\n    struct extern_stub *stubs;\n    int                 nstubs;\n    int                *stub_for_elfsym;\n};\n\n/* Resolve relocations within `s` against the laid-out sections and\n * stubs. Modifies the section data buffer in place. Returns 0 on\n * success, -1 on error. */\nstatic int exe_resolve_section_relocs(TCCState *s1, Section *s,\n                                      uint32_t sect_vmaddr,\n                                      unsigned char *sect_data,\n                                      struct exe_reloc_ctx *ctx)\n{\n    struct exe_sect *all_sects = ctx->all_sects;\n    int nsec = ctx->nsec;\n    struct extern_stub *stubs = ctx->stubs;\n    int nstubs = ctx->nstubs;\n    int *stub_for_elfsym = ctx->stub_for_elfsym;"
}

## 🤖 Claude

Let me write up the session and commit:

---

## 🤖 Claude


🔧 Using tool: Write
Input: {
  "file_path": "/Users/cell/claude/tcc-darwin8-ppc/docs/sessions/023-macho-executable/README.md",
  "content": "# 023 \u2014 Mach-O executable output\n\n`tcc -o exe foo.c` now produces a Mach-O executable directly \u2014 no\n`gcc-4.0` link step. Two phases:\n\n## Phase A: Bare-bones MH_EXECUTE\n\nSelf-contained executables for programs that use no libSystem (or\nonly direct syscalls). Layout:\n\n```\n__PAGEZERO  vmaddr 0           vmsize 0x1000   no file content\n__TEXT      vmaddr 0x1000      file 0..N       header+cmds + .text + crt-shim\n__LINKEDIT  vmaddr next-page   file N..        symtab + strtab\nLC_SYMTAB, LC_UNIXTHREAD with srr0 = entry\n```\n\nThe crt-shim (4 instructions) is appended to .text:\n```\nbl   _main          ; r3 = main's return value\nli   r0, 1          ; SYS_exit\nsc                  ; trap to kernel\ntrap                ; defensive\n```\n\nInternal `R_PPC_REL24` / `R_PPC_ADDR16_HA` / `R_PPC_ADDR16_LO` /\n`R_PPC_ADDR32` relocations are resolved at link time.\n\n\u2713 Works: `int main(){return N;}`, `sq(7)`, `fact(5)`, recursive calls.\n\n## Phase B: libSystem stubs\n\nWhen the program references external functions (e.g. `printf`,\n`puts`, `getpid`), the EXE writer:\n\n1. Calls `collect_extern_stubs()` to enumerate undef-external function\n   references via REL24.\n2. Synthesizes `__TEXT,__symbol_stub` (16 bytes/stub, 4 instructions:\n   `lis r12, ha(slot); lwz r12, lo(slot)(r12); mtctr r12; bctr`).\n3. Synthesizes `__DATA,__nl_symbol_ptr` (4 bytes/stub) \u2014 initial 0,\n   filled by dyld at load.\n4. Adds `LC_LOAD_DYLINKER /usr/lib/dyld`.\n5. Adds `LC_LOAD_DYLIB /usr/lib/libSystem.B.dylib`.\n6. Adds `LC_DYSYMTAB` with indirect symbol table.\n7. Sets each undef symbol's `n_desc` = `(LIBRARY_ORDINAL=1) << 8` for\n   two-level namespace lookup.\n8. Uses an extended crt-shim (9 instructions, 36 bytes) that aligns\n   the stack to 32 bytes and sets up a back-chain word per Apple ABI\n   before `bl _main`.\n\nThe header flags are `MH_NOUNDEFS | MH_DYLDLINK | MH_TWOLEVEL`.\n\n\u2713 Works:\n- `getpid()`: returns pid (mod 256).\n- `write(1, \"hello\\n\", 6)`: prints \"hello\\n\".\n- `strlen`, `strchr`, `strcmp`: pure-function libSystem calls.\n\n\u2717 Fails (SIGBUS, exit 138):\n- `puts(\"hello\")` \u2014 touches `stdout` (a libSystem-internal `FILE*`).\n- `printf(...)` \u2014 same.\n- `getenv(\"HOME\")` \u2014 reads `_environ` (a libSystem global).\n- `malloc` \u2014 needs heap setup.\n- Anything that touches per-process libSystem state.\n\n## What's missing for full libSystem support\n\nThe same `puts` program **works** when linked via `gcc-4.0`. The\ndifference is `gcc-4.0` includes `/usr/lib/crt1.o` automatically.\n\nApple's `crt1.o` does considerable startup work that we don't\nreplicate:\n\n```\nstart:\n    ; align stack\n    ; set up r3=argc, r4=argv, r5=envp from kernel-passed r1\n    bl __start\n\n__start:\n    ; spill argc/argv/envp to libSystem globals (_NXArgc, _NXArgv, _environ)\n    ; call __dyld_func_lookup repeatedly to find dyld helpers\n    ; call dyld init helpers (image_state, etc.)\n    ; call __keymgr_dwarf2_register_sections (libgcc EH unwind tables)\n    ; call _atexit chain registration\n    ; call _main(argc, argv, envp)\n    ; call _exit(main's return)\n```\n\n`_NXArgc`, `_NXArgv`, `_environ` live in libSystem's `__DATA` segment,\naccessible via `__nl_symbol_ptr` indirection (the same machinery we\nuse for OBJ output). Without these set, libSystem's stdio and\nenvironment functions deref invalid pointers and crash.\n\n## Two paths to full libSystem support (deferred)\n\n1. **Implement a minimal Mach-O .o linker** in tcc that can read and\n   merge `/usr/lib/crt1.o` (or any `.o`) into our executable output.\n   This is the proper long-term path; it also enables linking\n   user-provided `.o` files into the final executable.\n\n2. **Hardcode a crt1-equivalent init stub** in `tcc/lib/lib-ppc.c`\n   that reads argc/argv/envp from r1, sets up the libSystem globals\n   via `__nl_symbol_ptr` references, and calls main. Smaller scope\n   than option 1 but doesn't help with linking other `.o` files.\n\nEither way, this is a follow-up session.\n\n## Test record\n\n| Test | Result |\n|---|---|\n| `int main(){return N;}` for various N | \u2713 |\n| `sq(7) = n*n` | \u2713 exit 49 |\n| `fact(5)` | \u2713 exit 120 |\n| `getpid()` | \u2713 |\n| `write(1, \"hello\\n\", 6)` | \u2713 |\n| `strlen(\"foo\") + strchr` | \u2713 |\n| `puts(\"hello\")` | \u2717 SIGBUS (libSystem state) |\n| `printf(\"hello %d\", 42)` | \u2717 SIGBUS (libSystem state) |\n| `getenv(\"HOME\")` | \u2717 SIGBUS (libSystem state) |\n| Same `puts` via tcc -c + gcc-4.0 link | \u2713 (gcc bundles crt1.o) |\n\n## Files changed\n\n- `tcc/ppc-macho.c`: +691 lines for `macho_output_exe` and helpers.\n\n## Decisions\n\n- **Non-lazy stubs (4-instruction load-and-jump) instead of Apple's\n  32-byte PIC lazy stubs.** Simpler; works for our purposes since\n  dyld resolves the slots eagerly (`S_NON_LAZY_SYMBOL_POINTERS`).\n  No need for `dyld_stub_binding_helper`.\n\n- **Two-level namespace by default** (`MH_TWOLEVEL`). Mirrors gcc-4.0\n  output and is the modern Mach-O convention.\n\n- **Single `LC_LOAD_DYLIB` for libSystem.** Not yet hooked up to\n  arbitrary `-l` flags; user-requested dylibs would be a separate\n  enhancement.\n\n## Next session\n\n024 \u2014 implement a Mach-O `.o` reader so we can pull in\n`/usr/lib/crt1.o` automatically when building executables. That\nunblocks full libSystem support, which is the path to a fully\nself-linked tcc-self.\n"
}

  "old_string": "| \u2705 | **`v0.1.0-g3` tarball \u2014 `/opt`-installable, ~146 KB, verified on iMac G3** ([022](docs/sessions/022-v0.1.0-g3-release/README.md)) |",
  "new_string": "| \u2705 | **`v0.1.0-g3` tarball \u2014 `/opt`-installable, ~146 KB, verified on iMac G3** ([022](docs/sessions/022-v0.1.0-g3-release/README.md)) |\n| \ud83d\udfe1 | Mach-O exec output \u2014 `tcc -o exe` works for syscall-only programs ([023](docs/sessions/023-macho-executable/README.md)); libSystem stdio needs crt1 |"

## 🤖 Claude


🔧 Using tool: Write
Input: {
  "file_path": "/tmp/commit_msg.txt",
  "content": "Mach-O executable output: Phase A + Phase B (023)\n\n`tcc -o exe foo.c` now produces a Mach-O executable directly \u2014 no\ngcc-4.0 link step required.\n\nPhase A (self-contained, no libSystem):\n* `__PAGEZERO` + `__TEXT` + `__LINKEDIT` segments.\n* 4-instruction crt-shim appended to .text: bl _main; li r0,1; sc; trap.\n* `LC_SYMTAB` + `LC_UNIXTHREAD` with srr0 = entry.\n* Resolves internal REL24 / ADDR16_HA/LO / ADDR32 relocations.\n\nPhase B (libSystem stubs via dyld):\n* `__symbol_stub` (4-instruction lis/lwz/mtctr/bctr per stub).\n* `__nl_symbol_ptr` (4-byte slot per stub, dyld fills at load).\n* `LC_LOAD_DYLINKER /usr/lib/dyld`.\n* `LC_LOAD_DYLIB /usr/lib/libSystem.B.dylib`.\n* `LC_DYSYMTAB` + indirect symtab.\n* `n_desc` carries LIBRARY_ORDINAL=1 for two-level namespace.\n* Extended crt-shim aligns r1 to 32 bytes per Apple ABI.\n\nTests: return-N programs, recursive functions, syscalls (write,\ngetpid), pure-function libSystem calls (strlen, strchr) all work.\n\nLimitation: programs that touch libSystem-internal state (printf,\nputs, malloc, getenv) still SIGBUS. Apple's crt1 sets up\n_NXArgc/_NXArgv/_environ + invokes dyld init helpers; we don't\nyet replicate that. Path to fix: implement minimal Mach-O .o\nreader so we can link in /usr/lib/crt1.o automatically. Deferred\nto session 024.\n\nCo-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>\n"
}

## 🤖 Claude

Substantial progress made on Mach-O executable output. Here's the final state:

**Self-hosting** ✅ (since session 020) — tcc-self compiles tcc.c → tcc-self2 byte-identical fixpoint.

**Linking (Mach-O exec output)** 🟡 — `tcc -o exe foo.c` now produces standalone Mach-O executables on Tiger PPC, no gcc-4.0 link step needed:

```
$ ./tcc -o /tmp/w /tmp/w.c              # write(1, "hello\n", 6)
$ /tmp/w
hello from tcc-emitted exec!
$ otool -L /tmp/w
    /usr/lib/libSystem.B.dylib (compat 1.0.0, current 88.0.0)
$ file /tmp/w
/tmp/w: Mach-O executable ppc
```

**What works:**
- Self-contained programs: `int main(){return N;}`, recursion, function calls.
- libSystem syscall wrappers: `write`, `read`, `getpid`, `exit`.
- Stateless libSystem fns: `strlen`, `strchr`, `strcmp`.

**What doesn't work yet:**
- libSystem stdio (`printf`, `puts`) and global-state fns (`getenv`, `malloc`) crash with SIGBUS.
- Reason: Apple's `crt1.o` does substantial setup (sets `_NXArgc`/`_NXArgv`/`_environ` libSystem globals, calls `__dyld_func_lookup` to bootstrap dyld helpers, registers libgcc EH tables, etc.). gcc bundles `crt1.o` automatically; we don't replicate this yet.

**Path to full linking:**
Implement a minimal Mach-O `.o` reader so tcc can pull in `/usr/lib/crt1.o` and merge it into the output. This is its own multi-session project (~weeks of work) — substantially larger than what fit in this session.

Commits this session:
- `a42c8f7` Phase A: standalone Mach-O exec for `return N` programs
- `26a8dcc` Phase B: libSystem stubs (works for syscalls + pure-fn libSystem)

The v0.1.0-g3 tarball still builds and works (verified end-to-end). Self-host fixpoint preserved. The user has working self-hosted tcc with optional standalone Mach-O exec output for the common cases that don't need libSystem stdio.

---

## 👤 User

This session is being continued from a previous conversation that ran out of context. The summary below covers the earlier portion of the conversation.

Summary:
1. Primary Request and Intent:
   - User initially: "continue unsupervised until we have complete self-hosting and linking" — close the gap from v0.1.0-g3 release where tcc compiles tcc-self correctly but still relies on gcc-4.0 for the link step.
   - Mid-session: "before we continue, was tcc designed to be its own linker on the other platforms, or is this a darwin/ppc specific thing we are doing? is there any disadvantage to using gcc's linker?" — wanted explanation of the design context.
   - "excellent. can you capture that? useful context. please proceed" — capture the design discussion in repo docs.
   - "let's go with 1" — choose option 1 from prior summary: implement a classic-Mach-O .o reader so tcc can pull in /usr/lib/crt1.o and link executables fully without gcc-4.0.
   - Final: "sorry to interrupt, we are nearly out of context. can you write a handoff doc? also, can we switch to using ibookg37 for development? it is 300MHz faster than imacg3."

2. Key Technical Concepts:
   - tcc as a self-contained toolchain (compiler + linker) on Linux ELF (tccelf.c), Windows PE (tccpe.c), modern macOS (tccmacho.c)
   - Classic Mach-O format (vs modern LC_DYLD_CHAINED_FIXUPS used by tccmacho.c)
   - MH_OBJECT (filetype=1), MH_EXECUTE (=2), magic 0xfeedface (32-bit BE)
   - Load commands: LC_SEGMENT, LC_SYMTAB, LC_DYSYMTAB, LC_LOAD_DYLINKER, LC_LOAD_DYLIB, LC_TWOLEVEL_HINTS (=0x16), LC_UNIXTHREAD
   - PPC_THREAD_STATE: 40 32-bit words; srr0 at index 0 (entry PC)
   - nlist (12 bytes: n_strx, n_type, n_sect, n_desc, n_value)
   - Indirect symbol table for S_SYMBOL_STUBS / S_NON_LAZY_SYMBOL_POINTERS / S_LAZY_SYMBOL_POINTERS
   - PPC relocations: VANILLA, BR24/JBSR (REL24), HA16/HI16/LO16 (with PAIR), SECTDIFF (scattered)
   - PIC indirection: per-function r30 anchor via `bcl 20,31,.+4 ; mflr r30`; HA16_PIC/LO16_PIC encode (slot - anchor) displacement
   - Two-level namespace: n_desc high byte = LIBRARY_ORDINAL (1 for first LC_LOAD_DYLIB)
   - libSystem internals: __sF (FILE pool), _NXArgc/_NXArgv/_environ (defined by crt1.o, NOT exported by libSystem), _NSGetEnviron (in dyld), __mod_init_func constructors
   - crt1.o: defines start, __start, dyld_stub_binding_helper, __dyld_func_lookup; chains through dyld helpers to initialize libSystem
   - tcc-internal model: ELF-style sections (s1->sections), symbols (s1->symtab), relocations via put_elf_reloc
   - AFF_BINTYPE_REL (1), DYN (2), AR (3), C67 (4); added MACHO_REL (5)

3. Files and Code Sections:
   - **tcc/tcc.h**: Added `#define AFF_BINTYPE_MACHO_REL 5     /* classic Mach-O .o (MH_OBJECT) */` and forward declaration `ST_FUNC int macho_load_object_file(TCCState *s1, int fd, unsigned long file_offset);` under `#ifdef TCC_TARGET_PPC`.

   - **tcc/tccelf.c::tcc_object_type**: Extended with Mach-O detection at end:
     ```c
     #if defined(TCC_TARGET_MACHO) && defined(TCC_TARGET_PPC)
         if (size >= 16) {
             const unsigned char *b = (const unsigned char *)h;
             uint32_t magic = ((uint32_t)b[0]<<24)|((uint32_t)b[1]<<16)
                            | ((uint32_t)b[2]<<8) |((uint32_t)b[3]);
             uint32_t ftype = ((uint32_t)b[12]<<24)|((uint32_t)b[13]<<16)
                            | ((uint32_t)b[14]<<8) |((uint32_t)b[15]);
             if (magic == 0xfeedface && ftype == 1 /* MH_OBJECT */)
                 return AFF_BINTYPE_MACHO_REL;
         }
     #endif
     ```
   - **tcc/tccelf.c::tcc_output_file**: For PPC EXE, auto-injects __tcc_start_main via tcc_compile_string:
     ```c
     static const char start_src[] =
         "int    NXArgc  = 0;\n"
         "char **NXArgv  = 0;\n"
         "char **environ = 0;\n"
         "int  main(int argc, char **argv, char **envp);\n"
         "void exit(int);\n"
         "void __tcc_start_main(int argc, char **argv, char **envp){\n"
         "    NXArgc  = argc;\n"
         "    NXArgv  = argv;\n"
         "    environ = envp;\n"
         "    exit(main(argc, argv, envp));\n"
         "}\n";
     ```

   - **tcc/libtcc.c::tcc_add_binary**: Added dispatch:
     ```c
     #if defined(TCC_TARGET_MACHO) && defined(TCC_TARGET_PPC)
         case AFF_BINTYPE_MACHO_REL:
             ret = macho_load_object_file(s1, fd, 0);
             break;
     #endif
     ```

   - **tcc/ppc-macho.c**: ~1500+ lines added across the session covering:
     - Phase A+B EXE writer: macho_output_exe with __PAGEZERO/__TEXT/__DATA/__LINKEDIT segments, crt-shim, non-lazy stubs (lis/lwz/mtctr/bctr), __nl_symbol_ptr slots
     - Extern-data PIC support via collect_extern_nlptrs and exe_resolve_section_relocs PIC handling
     - __mh_execute_header N_ABS sym, LC_TWOLEVEL_HINTS, __DATA,__dyld section
     - Mach-O .o reader (session 025): macho_load_object_file, macho_section_to_elf, macho_translate_sym, macho_translate_relocs, macho_resolve_stub_slot, mach_get32/16, struct mach_section/mach_nlist/macho_load_ctx
     - Section name mapping: __TEXT,__text → .text; __TEXT,__const|__cstring → .rodata; __DATA,__data|__dyld → .data; __DATA,__bss → .bss; skip stub/la_ptr sections
   
   - **tcc/ppc-gen.c**: Extended `ppc_need_pic_for_sym` to fire for TCC_OUTPUT_EXE in addition to OBJ; same for PIC base setup gate in gfunc_prolog.

   - **tcc/lib/lib-ppc.c**: Documentation comment block for __tcc_start_main (actual function injected by tcc_output_file).

   - **README.md**: Updated build instructions to use `ssh ibookg37` and noted "Primary dev host is ibookg37 (300 MHz faster than the iMac G3 the project started on; both run Tiger 10.4.11 with the same gcc-4.0 + libSystem + dyld)."

   - **docs/notes/linker-design.md** (new, 92 lines): Captures that tcc IS designed as its own linker on every platform; lists disadvantages of leaning on gcc-4.0 ld (dependency, speed, self-host purity, minimal Tiger bootstrap); sketches the path to closing the gap (Mach-O .o reader).

   - **docs/sessions/023-macho-executable/README.md**: Phase A + B writeup
   - **docs/sessions/024-libsystem-init/README.md**: extern-data PIC, __tcc_start_main, libSystem-init mystery
   - **docs/sessions/025-macho-o-reader/HANDOFF.md** (new): comprehensive handoff doc detailing what's done, what's blocking (MH_OBJECT detection not firing at runtime), debug approach, other rough edges, test recipe

4. Errors and fixes:
   - **srr0 thread-state index off-by-many**: My initial guess of index 38 (then 0) was wrong; debugged by writing `0xa0000000 | wi` to every position and inspecting otool output. Final: srr0 at index 0 (start of array).
   - **rlwinm encoding bug**: encoded as 0x54210036 (ME=27) instead of 0x54210034 (ME=26); fixed by recomputing.
   - **`_main` vs `main` symbol lookup**: tcc adds leading underscore for Mach-O. Fixed by searching for both names.
   - **9-arg function exceeded PPC's 8-GPR call limit**: bundled exe_resolve_section_relocs args into `struct exe_reloc_ctx`.
   - **Nested /* */ comment in function header**: had `/* offset within section */` inside a `/* ... */` block. Fixed by changing to `-- offset within section`. This was the cause of mysterious "parse error before 'if'", "data definition has no type or storage class" errors at random downstream lines.
   - **Mid-block declarations with -Wdeclaration-after-statement**: moved all declarations to block tops to satisfy C89-strict mode.
   - **dyld: Symbol not found: __NXArgc**: had declared `extern int _NXArgc` in C source, tcc added another underscore making `__NXArgc`. Fixed by using `extern int NXArgc` (tcc adds the leading _).
   - **dyld: Symbol not found: _NXArgc** (in libSystem): turned out _NXArgc is NOT exported by libSystem; it's defined by crt1.o. Fixed by DEFINING (not extern-ing) the globals in our injected source with `int NXArgc = 0;` etc.
   - **__mh_execute_header missing**: Added as N_ABS|N_EXT symbol pointing at __TEXT segment base.
   - **libSystem stdio still crashes** (puts/printf SIGBUS): root cause is dyld not invoking libSystem's __mod_init_func. Tried LC_TWOLEVEL_HINTS, MH_BINDATLOAD, __DATA,__dyld section — none fixed it. This is THE current blocker that crt1.o-via-Mach-O-.o-reader is meant to solve.

5. Problem Solving:
   - Solved: Phase A executable output for self-contained programs
   - Solved: Phase B for syscall-only and pure-fn libSystem programs
   - Solved: Extern-data PIC indirection works in EXE mode
   - Solved: Symbol table exports all globals so dyld can find _NXArgc etc.
   - Ongoing: dyld doesn't run libSystem __mod_init_func for our binaries; the proper fix is linking in /usr/lib/crt1.o via the Mach-O .o reader currently being implemented
   - Blocking: tcc_object_type's MH_OBJECT detection added but not firing at runtime; passing /usr/lib/crt1.o still hits "unrecognized file type"

6. All user messages:
   - "excellent, please continue unsupervised until we have complete self-hosting and linking"
   - "excellent. before we continue, was tcc designed to be its own linker on the other platforms, or is this a darwin/ppc specific thing we are doing? is there any disadvantage to using gcc's linker?"

   - "let's go with 1"
   - "sorry to interrupt, we are nearly out of context. can you write a handoff doc? also, can we switch to using ibookg37 for development? it is 300MHz faster than imacg3."

7. Pending Tasks:

Confirm `TCC_TARGET_MACHO` and `TCC_TARGET_PPC` are both defined when tccelf.c is compiled (`grep '#define TCC_TARGET_' tcc/config.h`).    - **Handle local-section reloc targets** in macho_translate_relocs (currently dropped via `else { continue; }`). Will hurt crt1.o's `bl _start → __start` REL24.
   - **Gate __tcc_start_main injection** when crt1 is loaded (skip if `_start` already in symtab).
   - **Auto-add /usr/lib/crt1.o** for TCC_OUTPUT_EXE on PPC.
   - **Update entry-point selection** in macho_output_exe to prefer crt1's `start` when present.

   - **End-to-end test**: full self-link of `printf("hello")` program via tcc alone (no gcc), then full tcc-self self-link.

🔧 Using tool: Edit
Input: {
  "replace_all": false,
  "file_path": "/Users/cell/claude/tcc-darwin8-ppc/tcc/tccelf.c",
  "old_string": "#if defined(TCC_TARGET_MACHO) && defined(TCC_TARGET_PPC)\n    /* Classic Mach-O on big-endian PPC: magic 0xfeedface (read as\n     * `0xfe 0xed 0xfa 0xce` byte sequence). filetype is the 4th\n     * 32-bit big-endian word (offset 12). */\n    if (size >= 16) {\n        const unsigned char *b = (const unsigned char *)h;\n        uint32_t magic = ((uint32_t)b[0]<<24)|((uint32_t)b[1]<<16)\n                       | ((uint32_t)b[2]<<8) |((uint32_t)b[3]);\n        uint32_t ftype = ((uint32_t)b[12]<<24)|((uint32_t)b[13]<<16)\n                       | ((uint32_t)b[14]<<8) |((uint32_t)b[15]);\n        if (magic == 0xfeedface && ftype == 1 /* MH_OBJECT */)\n            return AFF_BINTYPE_MACHO_REL;\n    }\n#endif\n    return 0;\n}",
  "new_string": "#if defined(TCC_TARGET_MACHO) && defined(TCC_TARGET_PPC)\n    /* Classic Mach-O on big-endian PPC: magic 0xfeedface (read as\n     * `0xfe 0xed 0xfa 0xce` byte sequence). filetype is the 4th\n     * 32-bit big-endian word (offset 12).\n     *\n     * Apple distributes /usr/lib/crt1.o (and friends) as fat\n     * (universal) binaries \u2014 magic 0xcafebabe + nfat_arch + N\n     * fat_arch records (20 bytes each: cputype/subtype/offset/\n     * size/align, big-endian). We look up the ppc slice\n     * (cputype=18) and peek at its embedded mach_header to confirm\n     * MH_OBJECT. */\n    if (size >= 16) {\n        const unsigned char *b = (const unsigned char *)h;\n        uint32_t magic = ((uint32_t)b[0]<<24)|((uint32_t)b[1]<<16)\n                       | ((uint32_t)b[2]<<8) |((uint32_t)b[3]);\n        uint32_t ftype = ((uint32_t)b[12]<<24)|((uint32_t)b[13]<<16)\n                       | ((uint32_t)b[14]<<8) |((uint32_t)b[15]);\n        if (magic == 0xfeedface && ftype == 1 /* MH_OBJECT */)\n            return AFF_BINTYPE_MACHO_REL;\n        if (magic == 0xcafebabe) {\n            uint32_t nfat = ((uint32_t)b[4]<<24)|((uint32_t)b[5]<<16)\n                          | ((uint32_t)b[6]<<8) |((uint32_t)b[7]);\n            uint32_t i;\n            unsigned char fa[20];\n            unsigned char hh[16];\n            uint32_t cputype, off2;\n            uint32_t m2, ft2;\n            if (nfat > 32) nfat = 32;\n            for (i = 0; i < nfat; i++) {\n                if (lseek(fd, 8 + (off_t)i * 20, SEEK_SET) < 0) break;\n                if (full_read(fd, fa, 20) != 20) break;\n                cputype = ((uint32_t)fa[0]<<24)|((uint32_t)fa[1]<<16)\n                        | ((uint32_t)fa[2]<<8) |((uint32_t)fa[3]);\n                off2 = ((uint32_t)fa[8]<<24)|((uint32_t)fa[9]<<16)\n                     | ((uint32_t)fa[10]<<8)|((uint32_t)fa[11]);\n                if (cputype != 18 /* CPU_TYPE_POWERPC */) continue;\n                if (lseek(fd, off2, SEEK_SET) < 0) continue;\n                if (full_read(fd, hh, 16) != 16) continue;\n                m2 = ((uint32_t)hh[0]<<24)|((uint32_t)hh[1]<<16)\n                   | ((uint32_t)hh[2]<<8) |((uint32_t)hh[3]);\n                ft2 = ((uint32_t)hh[12]<<24)|((uint32_t)hh[13]<<16)\n                    | ((uint32_t)hh[14]<<8)|((uint32_t)hh[15]);\n                if (m2 == 0xfeedface && ft2 == 1)\n                    return AFF_BINTYPE_MACHO_REL;\n            }\n        }\n    }\n#endif\n    return 0;\n}"
}


---

## 🤖 Claude

Now update `macho_load_object_file` to detect fat magic and seek to the ppc slice:

---

## 🤖 Claude


🔧 Using tool: Edit
Input: {
  "replace_all": false,
  "file_path": "/Users/cell/claude/tcc-darwin8-ppc/tcc/ppc-macho.c",
  "old_string": "    /* Read the whole file into memory. */\n    lseek(fd, 0, SEEK_END);\n    fsize = lseek(fd, 0, SEEK_CUR);\n    lseek(fd, file_offset, SEEK_SET);\n    if (fsize <= (off_t)file_offset) {\n        tcc_error_noabort(\"macho: empty file\");\n        return -1;\n    }\n    file = tcc_malloc(fsize - file_offset);\n    if (full_read(fd, file, fsize - file_offset) != (ssize_t)(fsize - file_offset)) {\n        tcc_error_noabort(\"macho: short read\");\n        goto cleanup;\n    }\n    ctx.file = file;\n    ctx.file_size = fsize - file_offset;\n\n    if (ctx.file_size < 28) {\n        tcc_error_noabort(\"macho: file too small for header\");\n        goto cleanup;\n    }\n\n    magic = mach_get32(file);\n    if (magic != 0xfeedface) {\n        tcc_error_noabort(\"macho: bad magic 0x%x\", magic);\n        goto cleanup;\n    }",
  "new_string": "    /* Read the whole file into memory. */\n    lseek(fd, 0, SEEK_END);\n    fsize = lseek(fd, 0, SEEK_CUR);\n    lseek(fd, file_offset, SEEK_SET);\n    if (fsize <= (off_t)file_offset) {\n        tcc_error_noabort(\"macho: empty file\");\n        return -1;\n    }\n\n    /* Fat (universal) binary handling. /usr/lib/crt1.o on Tiger is a\n     * 4-arch fat archive (ppc, i386, ppc64, x86_64). Detect 0xcafebabe\n     * at the slice start, find the ppc slice, and shift file_offset\n     * to the slice's offset before doing anything else. */\n    {\n        unsigned char hdr[8];\n        uint32_t fat_magic;\n        lseek(fd, file_offset, SEEK_SET);\n        if (full_read(fd, hdr, 8) == 8) {\n            fat_magic = ((uint32_t)hdr[0]<<24)|((uint32_t)hdr[1]<<16)\n                      | ((uint32_t)hdr[2]<<8) |((uint32_t)hdr[3]);\n            if (fat_magic == 0xcafebabe) {\n                uint32_t nfat = ((uint32_t)hdr[4]<<24)|((uint32_t)hdr[5]<<16)\n                              | ((uint32_t)hdr[6]<<8) |((uint32_t)hdr[7]);\n                uint32_t j, ppc_off = 0, ppc_size = 0;\n                unsigned char fa[20];\n                if (nfat > 32) nfat = 32;\n                for (j = 0; j < nfat; j++) {\n                    uint32_t cputype, off2, sz2;\n                    if (lseek(fd, file_offset + 8 + (off_t)j * 20, SEEK_SET) < 0) break;\n                    if (full_read(fd, fa, 20) != 20) break;\n                    cputype = ((uint32_t)fa[0]<<24)|((uint32_t)fa[1]<<16)\n                            | ((uint32_t)fa[2]<<8) |((uint32_t)fa[3]);\n                    off2 = ((uint32_t)fa[8]<<24)|((uint32_t)fa[9]<<16)\n                         | ((uint32_t)fa[10]<<8)|((uint32_t)fa[11]);\n                    sz2 = ((uint32_t)fa[12]<<24)|((uint32_t)fa[13]<<16)\n                        | ((uint32_t)fa[14]<<8)|((uint32_t)fa[15]);\n                    if (cputype == 18 /* CPU_TYPE_POWERPC */) {\n                        ppc_off = off2;\n                        ppc_size = sz2;\n                        break;\n                    }\n                }\n                if (!ppc_size) {\n                    tcc_error_noabort(\"macho: fat binary has no ppc slice\");\n                    return -1;\n                }\n                file_offset += ppc_off;\n                fsize = (off_t)file_offset + ppc_size;\n            }\n        }\n        lseek(fd, file_offset, SEEK_SET);\n    }\n\n    file = tcc_malloc(fsize - file_offset);\n    if (full_read(fd, file, fsize - file_offset) != (ssize_t)(fsize - file_offset)) {\n        tcc_error_noabort(\"macho: short read\");\n        goto cleanup;\n    }\n    ctx.file = file;\n    ctx.file_size = fsize - file_offset;\n\n    if (ctx.file_size < 28) {\n        tcc_error_noabort(\"macho: file too small for header\");\n        goto cleanup;\n    }\n\n    magic = mach_get32(file);\n    if (magic != 0xfeedface) {\n        tcc_error_noabort(\"macho: bad magic 0x%x\", magic);\n        goto cleanup;\n    }"
}

## 🤖 Claude

Good. Now gate the injection on the absence of `_start` (crt1's entry-point label):

---

## 🤖 Claude


🔧 Using tool: Edit
Input: {
  "replace_all": false,
  "file_path": "/Users/cell/claude/tcc-darwin8-ppc/tcc/tccelf.c",
  "old_string": "    if (s->output_type == TCC_OUTPUT_EXE) {\n        /* `NXArgc`, `NXArgv`, `environ` are NOT exported by libSystem\n         * \u2014 they're conventionally DEFINED by /usr/lib/crt1.o, and\n         * dyld's `_NSGetEnviron`/`_NSGetArgv` find them in the\n         * executable image. We define them ourselves here so libSystem\n         * stdio/getenv/etc. work. (tcc's `s->leading_underscore` mode\n         * adds the leading `_`, so the on-disk symbols are `_NXArgc`,\n         * `_NXArgv`, `_environ` \u2014 matching what crt1.o would emit.) */\n        /* Use `= 0` initializers so these land in .data (initialized)\n         * rather than .bss/COMMON. Our EXE writer doesn't yet\n         * synthesize __bss/__common for executables, so .data is\n         * the path of least resistance. */\n        static const char start_src[] =\n            \"int    NXArgc  = 0;\\n\"\n            \"char **NXArgv  = 0;\\n\"\n            \"char **environ = 0;\\n\"\n            \"int  main(int argc, char **argv, char **envp);\\n\"\n            \"void exit(int);\\n\"\n            \"void __tcc_start_main(int argc, char **argv, char **envp){\\n\"\n            \"    NXArgc  = argc;\\n\"\n            \"    NXArgv  = argv;\\n\"\n            \"    environ = envp;\\n\"\n            \"    exit(main(argc, argv, envp));\\n\"\n            \"}\\n\";\n        if (tcc_compile_string(s, start_src) != 0)\n            return -1;\n    }",
  "new_string": "    if (s->output_type == TCC_OUTPUT_EXE) {\n        /* `NXArgc`, `NXArgv`, `environ` are NOT exported by libSystem\n         * \u2014 they're conventionally DEFINED by /usr/lib/crt1.o, and\n         * dyld's `_NSGetEnviron`/`_NSGetArgv` find them in the\n         * executable image. We define them ourselves here so libSystem\n         * stdio/getenv/etc. work. (tcc's `s->leading_underscore` mode\n         * adds the leading `_`, so the on-disk symbols are `_NXArgc`,\n         * `_NXArgv`, `_environ` \u2014 matching what crt1.o would emit.)\n         *\n         * GATE: skip injection if `_start` (crt1's primary symbol) is\n         * already present \u2014 that means the user supplied\n         * /usr/lib/crt1.o (or it was auto-added) and we'd duplicate\n         * its definitions. */\n        /* Use `= 0` initializers so these land in .data (initialized)\n         * rather than .bss/COMMON. Our EXE writer doesn't yet\n         * synthesize __bss/__common for executables, so .data is\n         * the path of least resistance. */\n        if (!find_elf_sym(s->symtab, \"_start\")) {\n            static const char start_src[] =\n                \"int    NXArgc  = 0;\\n\"\n                \"char **NXArgv  = 0;\\n\"\n                \"char **environ = 0;\\n\"\n                \"int  main(int argc, char **argv, char **envp);\\n\"\n                \"void exit(int);\\n\"\n                \"void __tcc_start_main(int argc, char **argv, char **envp){\\n\"\n                \"    NXArgc  = argc;\\n\"\n                \"    NXArgv  = argv;\\n\"\n                \"    environ = envp;\\n\"\n                \"    exit(main(argc, argv, envp));\\n\"\n                \"}\\n\";\n            if (tcc_compile_string(s, start_src) != 0)\n                return -1;\n        }\n    }"
}
